docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md:233:3. **Cross-cycle (decompose):** If the reframe attempt fails or produces no change, DDx dispatches a strong-power decomposer agent to split the bead into 2–5 executable child beads. If child depth is exhausted, the decomposer switches to sibling or replacement bead specs under the nearest safe parent/root. The oversized bead is left `status=open` with dependency, `execution-eligible=false`, or supersession metadata that lets the queue advance through the generated executable work.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:9:{"acceptance":"1. `ddxroot.GlobalDir()` returns `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global`, honoring an overridden XDG_DATA_HOME, verified by TestGlobalDir_HonorsXDGDataHome.\n2. `registry.ResolvePlugin` returns layer \"project\" with the project path when both project and global copies exist — TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal asserts the project copy wins.\n3. `registry.ResolvePlugin` returns layer \"global\" with the global path when the project copy is absent but the global copy exists — TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal.\n4. `registry.ResolvePlugin` returns layer \"baked-in\" for the default `ddx` plugin when neither project nor global copy exists, and a non-nil error for any other unresolved name — TestPluginLookup_BakedInDefaultOnly.\n5. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/ddxroot/... ./internal/registry/... green.\n6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"f4f79067f34de800edcb3151a2ed74b51407bb2b","created_at":"2026-06-02T01:50:49.073172731Z","description":"PROBLEM\nDDx has no runtime concept of a \"global\" plugin install nor a precedence order for resolving a plugin name. Before `--global` install (sibling bead) or `ddx init --global` (sibling bead) can exist, there must be a single resolver that, given a plugin name, returns its on-disk path by checking the project layer first, then a global layer, then the baked-in default.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/internal/registry/installer.go:66 `InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, src, projectRoot)` only knows the project root; there is no global target.\n- cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:75 `Path()` resolves only the project root (in-tree `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx` or convention `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/...` at line 86). There is no `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global` helper.\n- cli/internal/skills/install.go:28 `Install(src, projectRoot, opts)` and all callers assume a single project destination.\n- No `ResolvePlugin`-style function exists anywhere under cli/internal/registry.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. Add `GlobalDir()` to cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go returning `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global` (reuse the existing `xdgDataHome()` helper at ddxroot.go:89). The plugins root is `GlobalDir()/plugins`.\n2. Add `registry.ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name string) (path string, layer string, error)` implementing precedence:\n   - `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)/plugins/\u003cname\u003e` if the dir exists → layer \"project\".\n   - `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e` if it exists → layer \"global\".\n   - baked-in default (defaultplugin.FS, the `ddx` plugin only) → layer \"baked-in\"; return an error for any other unresolved name.\n3. Export the layer string so doctor/init/install (sibling beads) can report which layer answered.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Wiring `--global` into install/update commands (sibling bead: install/update --global).\n- `ddx init --global` and agent-tier symlinks (sibling bead: init topology).\n- `ddx doctor` reporting (sibling bead: doctor + specs).\n- Removing the existing per-project install. Both layers coexist.\n\nDEPS\nddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — `ddxroot.Path()` and the new `GlobalDir()` must share the same XDG_DATA_HOME convention agreed there. Sibling beads in this set depend on this resolver.","events_attachment":"ddx-04566a8f/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-04566a8f","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:config","area:plugin","area:cli","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-20de9a81","status":"closed","title":"registry: add two-layer plugin resolution (project → global → baked-in) with global XDG path helper","updated_at":"2026-06-02T01:56:37.269486624Z"}

It does not look for open beads X with superseded-by==Y and cascade-close them. FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation now requires this cascade with a strict scope guard.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nExtend cli/internal/bead/store.go Close(Y) with a post-close cascade step. After Y closes, scan for open beads X where supersession-by-Y is the sole remaining reason to stay open. Strict scope contract (each must hold; if any fails, surface in ddx work focus Section A, do NOT cascade):\n- X is open AND (X.superseded-by == Y OR X carries label superseded-by:Y) AND Y is now closed.\n- X has no other open dependents (no open bead Z with Z.dependencies referencing X).\n- X has no open dependencies of its own beyond the supersession marker.\n- X has attempt_count == 0.\n- X has no operator notes added after the supersession marker timestamp.\n- X is not currently claimed or in-progress (no live claim_id).\n- X carries no manual-hold / no-auto-close / container override label.\n\nRe-read X's current tracker state inside the same Close(Y) invocation before mutating — a concurrent worker may have changed X since the scan started.\n\nIdempotent: re-running Close(Y) is a no-op for already-closed X.\nCycle-guarded: track visited IDs within one Close(Y) call; cascade is one hop deep. If X is a superseder for an open Z, Z's cascade is DEFERRED to a separate operator action (do not transitively cascade).\nAudit: emit a superseded_close event on X with body {closed_by_cascade_of: Y, reason: \"closed_as_superseded_via:Y\"}. Operators can grep events to reverse.\n\nAdd operator override flag at the work command surface in a separate bead (--no-auto-supersede-close); the Close() code path always runs the cascade unless the flag has been threaded through. For this bead, accept the cascade always firing; the flag wiring is the work-loop bead's responsibility.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Cascading transitively beyond one hop (deferred to a future bead if ever needed).\n- The work-loop wiring of --no-auto-supersede-close flag (separate bead).\n- RC-3 dead-intermediate cleanup walk-up (separate bead, depends on this one).\n\nPARENT AND DEPS\nParent: ddx-2a1ece2b. No deps.","events_attachment":"ddx-04e89823/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-04e89823","issue_type":"feat","labels":["kind:feat","area:bead-lifecycle","phase:2","spec:FEAT-010","spec:reliability-principles"],"parent":"ddx-2a1ece2b","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-45b3300d","status":"closed","title":"store: cascade-close superseded children inside Close()","updated_at":"2026-05-21T10:57:30.774464116Z","work-failed-routes":[{"actual_power":5,"at":"2026-05-16T18:03:07Z","count":1,"model":"Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit","provider":"grendel-omlx","reason":"provider_connectivity"}]}

It returns:\n- \u003cprojectRoot\u003e/.ddx — when that directory exists (in-tree mode)\n- ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/\u003chost\u003e/\u003cowner\u003e/\u003crepo\u003e/ — otherwise (convention mode)\n\nProject identity in convention mode is parsed from `git remote get-url origin`:\n- https://github.com/easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx\n- git@github.com:easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx\n- https://gitlab.example.com/team/repo → gitlab.example.com/team/repo\n- Local (no remote) → local/\u003cbasename\u003e-\u003cshort-hash-of-abs-path\u003e\n\nThe XDG project directory IS a git repository — `git init`ed on first bootstrap. All DDx state for that project (beads.jsonl, attachments, archive, plugins, etc.) lives there and is versioned. Operators can `git remote add` it to a server to share state with teammates.\n\nWORKTREE REGISTRY (required, not speculative)\n\nEach XDG project root maintains \u003cxdg\u003e/worktrees.json:\n\n  {\"paths\": [{\"path\": \"/Users/erik/Projects/ddx\", \"first_seen_at\": \"...\", \"last_seen_at\": \"...\", \"hostname\": \"...\"}], \"master\": \"/Users/erik/Projects/ddx\"}\n\n- The first worktree to bootstrap an XDG project is auto-marked master.\n- Subsequent worktrees register as non-master.\n- `ddx project worktree list` shows the registry.\n- `ddx project worktree master \u003cpath\u003e` re-pins.\n- Audit log entries (bead events with result_rev/base_rev) include the master path so downstream tools resolve commit SHAs to a known worktree.\n\nDDX_ROOT NEVER PUNNED — bootstrap is atomic, idempotent, and a no-op when the directory already exists. Two concurrent ddx invocations on the same project must not race-create or race-write worktrees.json. That leaves the same bead getting reclaimed forever while the children are structurally blocked.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` already has no-progress and candidate-skip machinery, but it does not check for the specific back-edge case where a fresh child carries the attempted bead in its deps. Without that guard, the worker continues to re-claim the parent instead of escalating.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nWhen a run ends with `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` and any freshly-created child still depends on the attempted bead, emit a durable `operator_attention` event with reason `parent_back_edge`, park the bead, and stop re-claiming it. .ddx/beads.jsonl:25:{"acceptance":"1. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd -run 'TestWork_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeQueueScan|TestTry_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeClaim|TestVersionWarnsStaleSourceBinary|TestCheckVersionGate' -count=1` passes.\n2. `TestWork_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeQueueScan` proves no bead is claimed and no readiness/run service call occurs when the DDx source tree is ahead of the binary commit.\n3. `TestTry_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeClaim` proves `ddx try \u003cid\u003e` fails before claim/attempt evidence when the same stale-source condition is present.\n4. `ddx work` and `ddx try` error text includes the project root, binary commit, source HEAD, and `make install` recovery instruction.\n5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","claimed-at":"2026-05-15T08:33:11Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1283521","closing_commit_sha":"55c22e74e2e4b9f5a012b806b153a9bb10144f17","created_at":"2026-05-15T03:40:47.108776748Z","description":"PROBLEM\nOperators can run `ddx work` from the DDx source checkout with an installed binary built from an older commit. That produced live workers still using old readiness/routing behavior even after the local source tree had been fixed. `ddx version` can warn, but `ddx work` and `ddx try` still proceed to claim beads and perform actual builds with stale code.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE\n- `cli/cmd/command_factory.go:603` implements `warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource`, but it is only called from the `version` command path at `cli/cmd/command_factory.go:511`.\n- `cli/cmd/command_factory.go:169` runs the global pre-run version gate, but it checks `.ddx/versions.yaml` semver only and does not block a stale installed binary inside the DDx source repository.\n- `cli/cmd/work.go:130` and `cli/cmd/try.go:116` resolve the project root for execution, but neither refuses to build from an older DDx binary when the project root is the DDx source repo.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nAdd an execution preflight for `ddx work` and `ddx try`: when running inside the DDx source repository, and the binary reports a concrete build commit that is an ancestor of the source checkout `HEAD` with one or more commits ahead, fail before queue scan, claim, readiness, or dispatch. Keep `ddx version`, `ddx doctor`, `ddx init`, `ddx upgrade`, `help`, and `completion` usable for recovery. Do not make this a warning for worker commands.\n\nIN-SCOPE FILES\n- `cli/cmd/command_factory.go`\n- `cli/cmd/work.go`\n- `cli/cmd/try.go`\n- `cli/cmd/version_gate_test.go`\n- `cli/cmd/work_test.go`\n- `cli/cmd/try_test.go`\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Do not change release semver gating against `.ddx/versions.yaml`.\n- Do not auto-run `make install` or mutate the operator's PATH.\n- Do not block dev binaries with `Version=dev` or binaries without a known commit.\n- Do not inspect workers from other projects; this preflight is scoped to the resolved project root.","events_attachment":"ddx-08b452ee/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-08b452ee","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:cli","area:execution","kind:bug","operator-ux","stale-binary"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-e7ee87b3","spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"work: fail fast on stale DDx source checkout binary","updated_at":"2026-05-15T08:50:28.713080378Z","work-heartbeat-at":"2026-05-15T08:33:11.191178921Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:26:{"acceptance":"1. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/bead -run 'TestWorkerClaimLeaseDoesNotMutateTracker|TestReadyExecutionExcludesFreshSidecarLease|TestReadyExecutionReclaimsStaleSidecarLease' -count=1` passes.\n2. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestWorkLoop_ClaimAndHeartbeatDoNotMutateTrackerBeforeOutcome|TestWorkerStatusSidecar_RecordsCurrentBeadAttemptAndLastActivity' -count=1` passes and asserts `.ddx/beads.jsonl` bytes are unchanged from before worker claim through pre-outcome heartbeat ticks.\n3. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd -run 'TestCleanupRunStateDoesNotDirtyTrackedRepo|TestInitGitignoreRunStateMigration' -count=1` passes and proves tracked legacy `.ddx/run-state.json` / `.ddx/run-state/*.json` are removed from the index or otherwise cleaned without leaving git status dirty.\n4. `rg -n 'work-heartbeat-at|claimed-at|claimed-pid|claimed-machine|claimed-session|claimed-worktree' cli/internal/bead cli/internal/agent` shows no production worker-live-state write to `.ddx/beads.jsonl`; compatibility reads for stale legacy rows are allowed only when documented in comments and tests.\n5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","claimed-at":"2026-05-15T09:27:44Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1518880","closing_commit_sha":"9ad9ffbcea30f5213fd41e6f009df42aaa769632","created_at":"2026-05-15T04:00:00.210724454Z","description":"PROBLEM\n`ddx work --watch` currently dirties tracked files while a worker is merely alive. Operators then see `.ddx/beads.jsonl` and `.ddx/run-state*` changes even when no durable bead outcome has happened, and concurrent watchers can race the same moving state.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE\n- `cli/internal/bead/store.go:869-925` `ClaimWithOptions` persists live claim state (`status=in_progress`, `owner`, `claimed-at`, `claimed-pid`, `claimed-machine`, `claimed-session`, `claimed-worktree`, `work-heartbeat-at`) into `.ddx/beads.jsonl` before touching the external lease.\n- `cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness.go:15-24` and `cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness.go:64-75` already write an external lease, but the lease only carries `bead_id`, `updated_at`, and `pid`, so operator-facing claim metadata still depends on tracker mutation.\n- `cli/internal/agent/run_state.go:100-125` writes live attempt state under `.ddx/run-state/` and `.ddx/run-state.json`; `cli/cmd/init.go:18-31` ignores these paths for new repos, but existing tracked run-state files still produce dirty deletions when cleanup runs.\n- `cli/internal/agent/worker_liveness_sidecar_test.go:22-28` currently documents the tracker as the durable claim marker; that contract now conflicts with the desired live-state policy.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nMove worker-owned live claim state fully into sidecar state. Extend the claim lease record to carry owner/session/worktree/machine/started_at/pid/updated_at and make the `ddx work` / `ddx try` worker claim path update only that lease until a terminal durable outcome occurs. `ReadyExecution` must exclude beads with a fresh sidecar lease and include beads whose sidecar lease is absent or stale. Manual tracker lifecycle operations can remain explicit tracker operations, but ordinary worker liveness must not dirty `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.\n\nAlso migrate run-state policy so live run-state files are never tracked: add a migration/cleanup path that removes tracked `.ddx/run-state.json` and `.ddx/run-state/*.json` from the git index, keeps the ignore rules, and proves cleanup does not create tracked deletions.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Do not change bead close/proposed/blocked/cancelled semantics.\n- Do not drop execution evidence or attempt metrics.\n- Do not remove operator visibility; surface live owner/bead/attempt from sidecars instead of the tracker.\n- Do not manually edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` in tests or implementation.","events_attachment":"ddx-092c4343/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-092c4343","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:execution","area:bead","kind:bug","operator-ux","dirty-tree"],"owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-c191e91a","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-72280e29","spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"work: keep live worker state out of tracked DDx files","updated_at":"2026-05-15T10:12:05.671061224Z","work-heartbeat-at":"2026-05-15T09:27:44.669641686Z"}

The regression suite includes TestDDxAgentModeGuidance_DocContract, TestDDxSkillQueueStewardRouting, TestExecuteBeadInstructionsContainsBeadExecutionMode, TestAgentRunRuntimePropagatesDDXMode, TestWorkFocusReportsInterventionQueue, and TestDDxWorkOnce_BeadExecutionIgnoresQueueStewardDefault.\n5. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... passes after the children land.\n6. lefthook run pre-commit passes after the children land.","created_at":"2026-05-07T18:22:59.417640633Z","description":"PROBLEM\nInteractive agents opened inside a DDx project can treat broad DDx prompts like \"what's next\", \"what's blocking the queue\", or \"do work\" as permission to start code edits. That competes with ddx work, which is supposed to drain execution-ready beads, and leaves no crisp default for human-required queue intervention, planning, and worker-capacity decisions.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nAGENTS.md:1-18 enters bead policy immediately without declaring a default interactive queue-steward posture. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:18-29 defines ddx run/try/work execution layers but not the interactive-agent boundary around them. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md:28-32 routes phrases like \"do work\" and \"what's on the queue\" through the ddx skill without distinguishing queue stewardship from bead execution. .agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:18-23 maps \"What should I work on next?\" to ddx bead ready, which exposes worker-ready beads rather than human-required intervention.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nDefine explicit DDx agent modes and implement the guardrails around them: queue_steward for broad interactive sessions, bead_execution for ddx work/try/execute-bead workers, direct_user_implementation for explicit human requests to edit code/docs, and review for read-only review asks. Add a focused operator command surface for intervention work and worker-capacity suggestions, while preserving ddx work as the owner of execution-ready implementation beads.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Replacing ddx work's existing execution loop.\n- Building a full TUI or chat UI.\n- Rewriting bead tracker storage by hand.\n- Changing merge-policy or tracker audit semantics.\n\nCHILD QUEUE\n- Spec guidance updates define the mode taxonomy and precedence.\n- DDx skill/AGENTS guidance makes queue stewardship the broad interactive default.\n- Agent execution plumbing marks ddx work/try prompts with bead_execution mode.\n- A command surface exposes human-required intervention and worker-capacity advice.\n- Regression tests and prompt evals prove interactive guidance does not suppress ddx work execution.","id":"ddx-09d7d787","issue_type":"epic","labels":["area:agent","area:cli","area:skills","kind:epic","operator-ux","phase:2","queue-steward","spec:FEAT-010","spec:FEAT-011"],"parent":"ddx-ac10e7af","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-010,FEAT-011","status":"open","title":"agent-modes: define queue-steward execution boundary","updated_at":"2026-05-07T18:22:59.417640633Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:30:{"created_at":"2026-05-11T19:59:02.143711599Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-0ab16765","depends_on_id":"ddx-c6317784","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T19:59:02Z"}],"description":"# PROBLEM\n`*Store` exposes 7 migration-tooling methods used by `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` and related admin commands: `MigrateLifecycle`, `MigrateFromHelix`, `MigrateToAxon`, `MigrateDryRun`, `MigrateLifecycleDryRun`, `DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired`, `ReconcileLifecycleMetadata`. These are admin/one-shot tooling, not steady-state Backend operations. After the BL bead moves concrete `*Store` to `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/`, `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` cannot reach these methods. They need an exposure path that doesn't violate the boundary lockdown.\n\n# ROOT CAUSE\n`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes the 7 migration methods. `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` imports `cli/internal/bead` and calls them on `*Store` concretely. Per TD-027 §11.5 + Migration row in the mutation rules table, these are admin tools not on the `Backend` interface. After BL, the only public surface in `cli/internal/bead/` is what's exported there — concrete `*Store` won't be reachable.\n\n# PROPOSED FIX\n\nPer the converged design recommendation (separate factory rather than `BeadMigration` sub-interface on Backend, to avoid forcing every backend to stub source-specific migrations):\n\n1. Create `cli/internal/bead/migrator.go` with a `Migrator` interface and `NewMigrator` factory:\n   ```go\n   type Migrator interface {\n       MigrateLifecycle(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)\n       MigrateLifecycleDryRun(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)\n       MigrateFromHelix(ctx context.Context, src string) error\n       MigrateToAxon(ctx context.Context, opts MigrateToAxonOptions) error  // deprecated; replaced by ddx-IMP\n       MigrateDryRun(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)\n       DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired(ctx context.Context) (bool, MigrationReport, error)\n       ReconcileLifecycleMetadata(ctx context.Context) (ReconcileReport, error)\n   }\n\n   func NewMigrator(opts MigratorOptions) (Migrator, error)\n   ```\n   The concrete implementation lives in `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` after BL; the public factory returns a `Migrator` interface.\n\n2. Move the 7 methods' bodies from `*Store` (in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` and `migrate.go`) onto an unexported `*storeMigrator` type that holds a `*Store` reference and implements `Migrator`. The `NewMigrator` factory returns this.\n\n3. Update `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` and any related admin commands:\n   ```go\n   mig, err := bead.NewMigrator(bead.MigratorOptions{Dir: workspace})\n   if err != nil { ... }\n   mig.MigrateLifecycle(ctx, opts)\n   ```\n\n4. Remove `*Store.MigrateLifecycle`, `MigrateLifecycleDryRun`, `MigrateFromHelix`, `MigrateToAxon`, `MigrateDryRun`, `DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired`, `ReconcileLifecycleMetadata` from the `*Store` public method set. Logic moves to the `*storeMigrator` type.\n\n5. `MigrateToAxon` retained as a deprecated method on `Migrator` that returns `ErrDeprecated` with a pointer to the IMP bead's new importer. Final removal happens after IMP lands.\n\n# NON-SCOPE\n- The new JSONL→Postgres importer (replaces `MigrateToAxon`'s JSONL-writing behavior) — separate IMP bead.\n- Schema-versioning migration ladder framework — deferred until first breaking change is proposed.\n- Other concerns — separate beads.\n\n# Acceptance criteria\n\n1. `cli/internal/bead/migrator.go` declares the `Migrator` interface (7 methods, ctx-aware) and `NewMigrator(opts) (Migrator, error)` factory. Compile-time assertion: `var _ Migrator = (*storeMigrator)(nil)`.\n2. `*Store` no longer exposes the 7 migration methods directly. Verified by `grep -E 'func \\(s \\*Store\\) (MigrateLifecycle|MigrateLifecycleDryRun|MigrateFromHelix|MigrateToAxon|MigrateDryRun|DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired|ReconcileLifecycleMetadata)\\b' cli/internal/bead/*.go` returning nothing.\n3. `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` constructs `Migrator` via `bead.NewMigrator(...)` and invokes operations. Holds `Migrator` interface, not `*storeMigrator` concrete.\n4. End-to-end CLI invocation works: `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --dry-run`, `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --apply` produce identical output to today. Test: `TestMigrator_LifecycleMigration_RoundTrip`, `TestMigrator_FromHelix_PreservesAllFields`.\n5. `MigrateToAxon` on the Migrator returns `ErrDeprecated` with message pointing at the new importer (will be implemented in IMP). Test: `TestMigrator_MigrateToAxon_ReturnsDeprecated`.\n6. Existing migration tests in `cli/internal/bead/migrate_test.go` continue to pass against the new Migrator-mediated path.\n7. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./...` is green.\n8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.\n\n# Labels\nphase:iterate, area:bead, area:migration, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027\n\n# Parent + Deps\nParent: ddx-8d747049\nDeps: ddx-c6317784.","events":[{"actor":"","body":"","created_at":"2026-05-14T04:10:22.980177713Z","kind":"cooldown-cleared","source":"ddx work clear-cooldowns","summary":"operator-bulk-clear"}],"id":"ddx-0ab16765","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:bead","area:migration","kind:refactor","phase:iterate","spec:TD-027"],"parent":"ddx-8d747049","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"status":"open","title":"MIG: Migration tooling factory — Migrator interface + NewMigrator + remove 7 *Store migration methods","updated_at":"2026-05-14T04:10:22.980177713Z"}

This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nThe decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:\n\n  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --\u003e child_A\n  e29892a4 -- parent --\u003e child_B\n  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           \u003c- BAD: parent is also a dep\n  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  \u003c- BAD\n\nThe parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.\n\nObserved scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):\n- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')\n- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)\n- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]\n- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores\n- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order\n\nRoot cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.\n\nThere is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nImplement only this child acceptance slice:\n- The orchestrator's 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning, when emitted alongside the parent-back-edge state, is upgraded from WARN to ERROR with the offending bead ID logged. Test: dispatch_logs_error_on_root_mutation_with_back_edge.\n- Lifecycle schema documents that 'parent' and 'deps' MUST be disjoint and that 'deps' MUST be acyclic with the parent-tree. docs/ddx/lifecycle-rules.md (or equivalent) updated; ADR file if no governing artifact already exists.\n- Existing valid graphs continue to pass 'ddx bead doctor' without --fix and without regression — regression test against a pre-existing tracker snapshot.\n\nNON-SCOPE\nDo not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows .ddx/beads.jsonl:37:{"closing_commit_sha":"rev-ddx-0c9f7c8a","created_at":"2026-05-21T03:26:17.932920891Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-0c9f7c8a","depends_on_id":"ddx-e663cb22","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-21T03:26:45Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM\ncli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:739 prepareDockerAttemptHome() copies host creds (~/.codex/auth.json, ~/.codex/config.toml, ~/.claude/.credentials.json) into ws.DockerHome under ws.DockerRun, which only reach the container via the /ddx-runtime HOST bind-mount (:636). On a de-nested/remote daemon that bind is invisible, so the agent inside the container has no credentials.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nDeliver credentials without a host bind: prefer key-based env injection (extend dockerCredentialEnv() to pass e.g. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY when present), and for file-based creds docker cp the prepared cred files into the container HOME after docker create / before start. Remove reliance on the /ddx-runtime bind for credential delivery.\n\nIN-SCOPE\n- cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go (prepareDockerAttemptHome, dockerCredentialEnv, DockerCloneAttemptBackend.Run cred step)\n- cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend_test.go\n\nOUT-OF-SCOPE\n- caches/runtime volumes (sibling bead)\n- workspace/ddx binary (keystone bead)\n\nACCEPTANCE\n1. Update TestPrepareDockerAttemptHomeCopiesMinimalAuth (attempt_backend_test.go:154) to reflect cp/env delivery rather than bind reliance.\n2. Add TestDockerCredentialDelivery_NoRuntimeBind asserting creds are delivered via env and/or docker cp targets (no dependence on a /ddx-runtime host bind).\n3. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... green.\n4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","events_attachment":"ddx-0c9f7c8a/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-0c9f7c8a","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:agent","kind:refactor","reliability","spec:FEAT-010"],"parent":"ddx-6967f6e6","priority":2,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"docker backend: inject agent credentials via env/docker cp instead of the runtime-home bind-mount","updated_at":"2026-05-28T16:52:40.806383629Z"}

It does not yet forbid direct field assignment of Profile/Harness/Model on Runtime structs inside run/try/work command code paths. The SD-024 runtimelint analyzer (cli/tools/lint/runtimelint/analyzer.go:41) forbids durable-knob fields on *Runtime structs generically, but does not name a per-command allowlist or produce a test that verifies the run/work/try paths specifically. No TestRoutingLint* test exercises this invariant.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n  - Extend routinglint (cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer.go) to flag direct Profile/Harness/Model field assignment on types matching *Runtime in cli/cmd/ run/work/try command files, or add a new rule in runtimelint covering this.\n  - Alternatively, add a targeted integration test TestRoutingLint_RunPathNeverSetsProfileDirectly that runs the analyzer against the live tree and asserts zero diagnostics for the assignment pattern.\n  - Fix any existing violations: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1893-1897 passes overrides.Profile/Harness/Model through CLIOverrides to config.LoadAndResolve — verify this is compliant (CLIOverrides is not a Runtime struct).\n\nNON-SCOPE\n  - .ddx/beads.jsonl:40:{"acceptance":"1. `TestExecutorLinuxPdeathsigKillsHarnessOnParentSIGKILL` starts an execute-bead executor path against a fake harness binary that forks a long-lived grandchild, SIGKILLs the parent worker process rather than sending graceful SIGTERM, and asserts the harness child and grandchild are gone within a bounded grace instead of becoming PID-1 orphans.\n2. `TestCmdSetProcessGroupLinuxSetsPdeathsigAndSetpgid` verifies the Linux executor command configuration includes both process-group isolation and a parent-death signal.\n3. `TestExecutorStartParentDeathSignalThreadGuard` or an equivalent focused test/rationale covers the `Pdeathsig` parent-thread caveat using `runtime.LockOSThread` or a parent-PID guard.\n4. `TestExecutorGracefulSignalStillTerminatesHarnessTree` proves existing SIGINT/SIGTERM or context-cancellation cleanup still terminates in-flight harness children.\n5. Non-Linux behavior is covered by `TestExecutorParentDeathSignalNonLinuxFallbackDocumented` or build-tagged documentation explaining the fallback and why `Pdeathsig` is unavailable.\n6. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/...` passes.\n7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"133c2ebe9d9d247d4384dfb3655d20abbae0b0c2","created_at":"2026-05-27T09:49:01.740020404Z","description":"PROBLEM\nWhen a `ddx work` orchestrator dies abnormally (SIGKILL, crash, OOM, or supervising-process loss) while an execute-bead attempt is running, the spawned harness child can reparent to PID 1 and continue indefinitely. Observed 2026-05-27: a `claude --print` execute-bead child remained orphaned with PPID 1 for ~9h53m after its `ddx work` parent exited.\n\nROOT CAUSE (file:line)\n- cli/internal/agent/executor.go:156 spawns the harness via `exec.Command(binary, args...)` and then calls `cmdSetProcessGroup(cmd)`.\n- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:13-15 sets only `SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true}`. There is no Linux `Pdeathsig`, so the kernel does not signal the harness when the parent dies abnormally.\n- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:20-25 kills the process group only from graceful executor paths.\n- cli/cmd/root.go:49-54 uses `signal.NotifyContext` for os.Interrupt and SIGTERM only. SIGKILL and crashes cannot be caught, so executor cleanup does not run.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nOn Linux, set `SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig` to SIGKILL or SIGTERM for execute-bead harness commands in `cmdSetProcessGroup`. Handle the Go runtime thread-migration caveat with `runtime.LockOSThread` around process start or an equivalent parent-PID guard so the parent-death signal is attached to the intended parent thread. Preserve process-group setup so existing graceful cancellation and timeout cleanup still kill the whole harness tree. Document the macOS/Windows fallback because `Pdeathsig` is Linux-only.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown already works through executor.go:231-255; do not regress it.\n- The harness-runner-internal leak in fizeau is covered by fizeau-50b6f055; this child bead is only the DDx orchestrator-to-harness-child boundary.\n- Startup orphan reaping is covered by a separate child bead from this decomposition.\n- docker-clone backend cleanup is covered by a separate child bead from this decomposition.\n\nCROSS-REF\n- fizeau-50b6f055: fizeau harness-runner process-group cleanup.\n- ddx-8f2e0ebf: lease release / operator-attention.","events_attachment":"ddx-0d7d5f0e/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-0d7d5f0e","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:execute-bead","area:workers","kind:bug","kind:reliability","priority:reliability","severity:high","observability"],"parent":"ddx-93d2ac52","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-f490694e","status":"closed","title":"fix executor: enforce parent-death cleanup for execute-bead harness children on Linux","updated_at":"2026-05-27T10:14:33.399641365Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:41:{"acceptance":"1. `git status --short cli/internal/server/frontend/build/.gitkeep` is empty after running `cd cli \u0026\u0026 make frontend-build` from a clean checkout.\n2. `make install` installs a DDx binary whose `ddx --version` output includes the current commit and does not include `-dirty`.\n3. `git check-ignore -v cli/internal/server/frontend/build/index.html cli/internal/server/frontend/build/.frontend-build-stamp` still shows generated frontend files are ignored.\n4. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"420a62c50968efe806abef6b500c6d56400aba00","created_at":"2026-05-15T23:00:27.142666011Z","description":"PROBLEM\nRunning `make install` from a clean DDx checkout builds and installs a binary stamped with `-dirty` even when no source edits were pending. The tracked `cli/internal/server/frontend/build/.gitkeep` currently contains a newline byte, but `touch` recreates an empty file after Vite removes the directory contents. That one-byte mismatch makes the source checkout dirty before `go build` computes `git describe --dirty` in `cli/Makefile:4-6`.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Normalize the tracked placeholder `cli/internal/server/frontend/build/.gitkeep` to the same empty-file content produced by the build target, or otherwise make `frontend-build` recreate the exact tracked bytes.\n- Verify `make install` from a clean checkout leaves no tracked `.gitkeep` diff and installs a DDx binary whose version string does not end in `-dirty`.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Do not track generated frontend build output under `cli/internal/server/frontend/build/`.\n- Do not change the frontend bundle, Vite config, or go:embed target.\n- Do not bypass the stale-binary guard in `ddx work`.\n\nDEPS\nNo deps. Under warn-only policy this skips every bead, idling a full fizeau queue (23 ready beads, 2026-05-18 05:27-05:39 UTC). This produces an unbreakable deadlock:\n\n  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --\u003e child_A\n  e29892a4 -- parent --\u003e child_B\n  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           \u003c- BAD: parent is also a dep\n  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  \u003c- BAD\n\nThe parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.\n\nObserved scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):\n- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')\n- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)\n- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]\n- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores\n- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order\n\nRoot cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.\n\nThere is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nImplement only this child acceptance slice:\n- Programmatic decomposition path (the action that fans a too-large bead into children) MUST NOT add the parent as a dep. Existing action templates / system prompts are audited and amended; one golden e2e test (decompose_does_not_self_dep) creates a bead with N children and asserts every child's deps set excludes the parent.\n- 'ddx bead doctor' (existing subcommand) gains a check 'parent_ancestor_in_deps' that scans the tracker, lists violating beads, and (with --fix) removes the offending edges. Output is stable / scriptable. Test: ddx_doctor_detects_and_repairs_back_edge.\n- ddx work no-progress remediation: when an attempted bead's outcome is 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale' AND any freshly-created child carries the attempted bead in its deps, ddx auto-emits an 'operator_attention' event with reason 'parent_back_edge' and stops re-claiming that bead. (Today it loops indefinitely.) Test: ddx_work_halts_on_parent_back_edge.\n\nNON-SCOPE\nDo not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. .ddx/beads.jsonl:51:{"acceptance":"1. `ddx init --global --silent` exits 0 and creates ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/ with the bundled library content.\n2. `ddx init --global` is idempotent: running twice does not error and does not destroy local-only files under global/.\n3. TestInitGlobal_IsIdempotent asserts a second run does not error and re-syncs the plugin content.\n5. TestConventionMode_ReadsGlobalSkillsBeforeHardcoded asserts that with --global installed, convention mode picks up the global plugin instead of the hardcoded one.\n6. ddx doctor's \"Global Install\" check exists and reports the install path + status.\n7. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... ./internal/config/... green.\n8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-15T01:09:39.694724565Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-15c71970","depends_on_id":"ddx-564ea1cf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-15T01:10:33Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-15c71970","depends_on_id":"ddx-ad338ec3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-15T01:10:34Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM\nConvention mode (per ddx-ad338ec3) bootstraps an XDG project dir but has no source for skills, personas, or the default ddx plugin. Either every project re-downloads the plugin on first use, or the user must have a global install. The latter is the better answer: install once per machine, all convention-mode projects share.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nThere is no per-user install path today. `ddx init` only writes per-project state. Skills/personas live in the project's .ddx/plugins/ (in-tree) or are missing entirely (convention).\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n\n1. Add `ddx init --global` flag (and corresponding code path in cli/cmd/init.go). When set:\n   - Resolve target path: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ (defaults to ~/.local/share/ddx/global/).\n   - Install the default ddx plugin (library/ contents) into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/.\n   - Write a default ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults (library URL, branch, etc.).\n   - Idempotent: re-running upgrades to a newer plugin version but does not destroy operator state.\n\n2. Convention mode reads ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ as the second precedence tier (in-tree \u003e global \u003e hardcoded). If --global has never been run, fall back to baked-in hardcoded defaults — convention mode still works without it; --global is an optimization for users who want offline-cacheable plugin content.\n\n3. Document the install path in ddx doctor: a new check \"Global Install\" reports whether ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ exists and points operators to `ddx init --global` if it does not.\n\n4. Do NOT remove the existing per-project plugin install (in-tree mode at .ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/). Both coexist.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- In-project ddx init manifest write (E4 bead).\n- Migration from legacy ~/.config/ddx/ or ~/.ddx/ if such exist on disk (none expected; today there is no global install).\n- Plugin update / upgrade flow for the global install (use existing `ddx update` mechanism; verify it works against the new path).\n\nDEPS\nddx-564ea1cf (E1) — resolver must exist.\nddx-ad338ec3 (E2) — convention mode reads from this path.","id":"ddx-15c71970","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:config","area:cli","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint"],"notes":"Superseded by epic restructure into 3 consolidated beads aligned to the two principles: (1) bulletproof ddx home resolution, (2) centralized vs in-project skills/personas. New beads pending.","parent":"ddx-602ae31a","priority":2,"schema_version":1,"status":"cancelled","title":"config: ddx init --global installs user-tier skills/personas to XDG global","updated_at":"2026-05-15T01:16:05.901133882Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:57:{"acceptance":"1. TestWorkWatch_ReexecsOnNewerInstalledBinaryBetweenBeads: with --watch, after a bead completes, if the installed binary commit differs from the running build commit, the worker re-execs (via an injectable re-exec seam) preserving argv; the next bead runs on the new binary.\n2. TestWorkWatch_DoesNotReexecMidBead: re-exec happens only at the between-bead boundary, never while a bead executes.\n3. TestWorkWatch_NoReexecWhenBinaryUnchanged: no re-exec when the installed binary matches the running build commit.\n4. A --self-refresh flag (default on for --watch) gates the behavior; --self-refresh=false disables it.\n5. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestWorkWatch_.*Reexec|SelfRefresh' -count=1 green.\n6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-06-06T15:53:49.368921604Z","description":"PROBLEM: `ddx work --watch` runs the binary it was launched with for its ENTIRE lifetime. When newer code is installed (the factory merges fixes + `make install`), the worker keeps executing STALE logic until manually killed+restarted. It only WARNS (\"installed ddx is built from X; source HEAD is Y (N commits ahead); run make install\") — no action. Observed live: a 2-worker drain ran ~75 commits behind, missing ALREADY-LANDED concurrency fixes (ddx-33530e68 post-land-outside-lock, ddx-0b659710 atomic-claim-release, ddx-274943fc worktree-leak) and repeatedly colliding on the durable-audit commit — the very fixes were sitting in source but not in the running binary. Operators must manually cycle workers. Operator expectation: \"the workers are supposed to know how to cycle automatically between tasks.\" (A stopgap supervisor that runs `ddx work --once` + `make install` between cycles is in place; this bead is the durable native fix.)\n\nROOT CAUSE: the --watch loop loads its binary once at startup; there is no between-task self-refresh / re-exec. The staleness warning (grep \"installed ddx is built from\" / \"source HEAD\") is informational only.\n\nPROPOSED FIX: At a SAFE boundary in the --watch loop — after a bead completes/unclaims and BEFORE claiming the next — detect whether the on-disk installed binary differs from the running process's build commit (compare the embedded build commit vs the installed binary's reported commit, or hash/stat the executable resolved via os.Executable/PATH). If newer, re-exec the process in place (syscall.Exec) on the fresh binary, preserving argv/flags (--watch, --min-power, etc.) so the next bead runs on the latest code. NEVER re-exec mid-bead. Gate behind --self-refresh (default ON for --watch). This delivers \"workers cycle automatically between tasks.\"\n\nNON-SCOPE: triggering `make install`/the build from the worker (separate concern — a supervisor/build pipeline produces the binary; this bead re-execs onto it); the durable-audit collision classifier (ddx-83361480/e8bf0695/2965925e).","events":[{"actor":"erik","body":"{\"bead_id\":\"ddx-171dc478\",\"detail\":\"resource_exhausted\",\"status\":\"resource_exhausted\"}","created_at":"2026-06-07T02:45:09.442808352Z","kind":"resource-exhausted","source":"ddx work","summary":"resource exhausted after cleanup; stopping work loop"},{"actor":"erik","body":"resource_exhausted\npowerClass=standard\noutcome_reason=unknown","created_at":"2026-06-07T02:45:09.524427854Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx work","summary":"resource_exhausted"}],"id":"ddx-171dc478","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:workers","kind:bug","kind:reliability","phase:iterate","spec:FEAT-010","incident:stale-binary-collision"],"parent":"ddx-3106674f","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"status":"open","title":"ddx work --watch: self-cycle between tasks — re-exec on a newer installed binary so workers never run stale code","updated_at":"2026-06-07T02:45:09.574437096Z"}

On 2026-05-15 the first attempt `20260515T081727-1e2bf23c` preserved a no-change decomposition/parent update result, and `ddx bead show ddx-c191e91a --json` reported `execution-eligible: false` plus labels `area:agent,area:execution,kind:cleanup,decomposed`. `ddx bead ready --execution` no longer listed the bead. Despite that, the same live watcher started a second implementation attempt at `20260515T082640-623a9c00` against the same parent, burning model time and showing false progress.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE\n`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1073-1113` claims the candidate selected from the queue snapshot without a post-selection guard that rechecks execution eligibility after tracker changes made by a prior attempt. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1681-1696` passes the originally selected `candidate` into `agenttry.Attempt`, so a stale candidate can still execute even if the store row has since been parked. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2367-2408` appends the terminal result and continues watch mode after a preserved/land-conflict/no-change result, allowing an immediate retry. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2603-2627` delegates selection to `PreviewQueue` and `ReadyExecution`, but the live incident proves this path can still select or retain an execution-ineligible decomposed parent. `cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:131-134` documents the intended invariant: successful decomposition creates children and sets the parent `execution-eligible=false`.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nAdd an execution-eligibility recheck immediately before claim and again before invoking `agenttry.Attempt` in the watch/drain loop. Ensure preserved no-change decomposition and mixed no-change/land-conflict outcomes do not allow the same live watcher to retry the parent in the same loop run.\n\nIN-SCOPE FILES\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_test.go, only if needed for fixture reuse\n\nNON-SCOPE\nDo not change merge policy or preserved-iteration storage. Do not re-enable execution of decomposed parents. Do not hide land conflicts generally. Do not close the parent automatically unless existing child-closure policy already authorizes it.\n\nDEPS\nNone. This is a worker-loop correctness bug independent of the child implementation beads for `ddx-c191e91a`.","events_attachment":"ddx-1df2c713/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-1df2c713","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:execution","kind:bug","operator-ux","downstream:fizeau"],"priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-d7461188","spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"work: stop retrying execution-ineligible decomposed parents","updated_at":"2026-05-15T15:14:04.623756947Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:74:{"acceptance":"1. `TestPreClaimReadinessCheck_VerdictAcceptsBoolStringAbsent` covers `verdict:true`, `verdict:false`, string verdicts including `\"ready\"`/`\"not_ready\"`, `null`, and absent verdict, and asserts the canonical decoded values.\n2. `TestReadinessChecksSchema` or an equivalent focused decoder/schema test proves the same verdict forms decode through the pre-claim readiness classification payload without producing `intake_error`.\n3. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestPreClaimReadinessCheck_VerdictAcceptsBoolStringAbsent|TestReadinessChecksSchema'` passes.\n4. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"26bb0aec290f51bdab758ee99be54d9840a57366","created_at":"2026-05-27T05:50:45.963362833Z","description":"PROBLEM\n`ddx work --watch` can reject every pre-claim readiness result when `readiness_checks[].verdict` is emitted as a JSON bool instead of the consumer's expected string shape. In the observed Fizeau queue, each ready bead produced the same `pre_claim_intake.warn` decode error and the worker idled indefinitely despite ready work.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE\nThe readiness classification decoder consumes `readiness_checks` through `preClaimReadinessCheck.Verdict` in `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:75-80`, then uses `failedReadinessReasons` in `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:823-834` to interpret failed checks. The contract must explicitly tolerate bool, string, null, and absent verdict values at this boundary; otherwise producer/consumer schema drift turns a readiness payload into `intake_error` before a bead can execute.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nImplement or verify a custom `readinessVerdict` decoder that accepts `true`, `false`, strings, `null`, and absent verdicts, coercing to a documented canonical form (`true` -\u003e `pass`, `false` -\u003e `fail`, string -\u003e trimmed string, null/absent -\u003e empty). Ensure the classification decode path exercises that type and that failed-check detection still treats canonical `fail` as the failure signal.\n\nNON-SCOPE\nDo not change Fizeau route or harness configuration. Do not implement same-fingerprint escalation, claim-success-rate warnings, CI schema locking, README documentation, lock handling from ddx-57c40485, or route-resolution wedge handling from ddx-8f2e0ebf. Do not rely on `/Users/erik/Projects/fizeau/.ddx/agent-logs/...` as load-bearing test input; inline fixtures in tests. Parent lifecycle, child dependency wiring, and tracker mutation are orchestrator-owned.\n\nGOVERNING ARTIFACT / CROSS-REF\nAR-2026-05-17 follow-up context; ADR-023 readiness warn-only policy; adjacent reliability bugs ddx-57c40485 and ddx-8f2e0ebf are cross-references only.\n\nDEPENDENCIES\nNone.","events_attachment":"ddx-1ecdeb6d/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-1ecdeb6d","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:execute-bead","area:pre-claim","area:workers","kind:bug","kind:reliability","observability","priority:reliability","severity:high","ac-quality:needs-refinement"],"parent":"ddx-f9ddaa68","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-4cec8a56","status":"closed","title":"Fix pre-claim intake verdict decoding for bool/string/absent readiness checks","updated_at":"2026-05-27T06:59:55.693755537Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:76:{"acceptance":"1. TestExecuteBeadInstructions_SerializesGitOperations asserts both instruction variants contain a direct prohibition on parallel git/staging/commit operations and require sequential git add/git commit handling.\n2. TestExecuteBeadInstructions_RequiresStagedPreCommitGate asserts both instruction variants say lefthook run pre-commit must be rerun after staging when staged files drive hooks and that no-staged-files runs are insufficient evidence.\n3. Prompt snapshot or guardrail coverage is updated so future prompt edits preserve both rules.\n4. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestExecuteBeadInstructions_(SerializesGitOperations|RequiresStagedPreCommitGate)|TestExecuteBeadInstructionsText' -count=1 passes.\n5. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... passes.\n6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"4be990cdd93a48ddec8392515627ec360d85decd","created_at":"2026-05-15T22:29:19.941605037Z","description":"PROBLEM\nDuring the current monitor on 2026-05-15, two Codex execute-bead workers recovered from avoidable tool-sequencing errors. For ddx-b6859802, the worker staged ordinary files and ignored evidence with parallel git add calls; one failed with: fatal: Unable to create ... index.lock: File exists. For ddx-bb8e7d07, the worker ran lefthook run pre-commit before staging and got a no-staged-files skip, then had to rerun after staging; it also attempted to interrupt a completed test-poll session. These recoveries waste worker time and can look like DDx dirty-tree failures even when the implementation is otherwise fine.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE\ncli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2337-2341 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2369-2373 tell rich-prompt and embedded-agent workers to run tests, stage specific paths, and commit once. They do not explicitly prohibit parallel git/index operations, do not say that lefthook run pre-commit must be run after staging when hooks are staged-file-sensitive, and do not warn that a no-staged-files hook run is insufficient acceptance evidence.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Update both executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText and executeBeadInstructionsAgentText to require git/staging/commit commands to run sequentially, not through parallel tool calls.\n- Require workers to rerun lefthook run pre-commit after staging the exact commit set when pre-commit depends on staged files; an earlier no-staged-files hook run is not sufficient AC evidence.\n- Add or extend prompt guardrail/snapshot tests in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_guardrails_test.go or cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_prompt_snapshot_test.go so the rule cannot regress.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Do not change core gitlock recovery.\n- Do not change execute-bead lifecycle, result schema, or merge policy.\n- Do not hand-edit .ddx/beads.jsonl.\n\nDEPS\nNone.","events_attachment":"ddx-213d7e95/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-213d7e95","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:2","area:agent","area:execution","kind:bug","operator-ux","dirty-tree","prevention"],"parent":"ddx-a9d130d0","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-ab0fbdd4","status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: serialize worker git commands and staged hook gates","triage.estimated_difficulty":"medium","updated_at":"2026-05-15T23:13:52.497835684Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:86:{"acceptance":"1. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestExecuteBead.*Cleanup|TestCleanupAttemptWorktree' -count=1` passes.\n2. A unit test exists for each of these non-success outcomes asserting WorktreeRemove was called: provider_connectivity, no_evidence_produced, execution_failed, structural_validation_failed, land_conflict, post_run_check_failed.\n3. A unit test asserts the success-path classifier does NOT call WorktreeRemove from the new hook (landing path retains ownership).\n4. A unit test asserts `ddx try --no-merge` preserves the worktree regardless of outcome.\n5. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./... -count=1` passes.\n6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"e979bcd786ff98bd4767596dd1ed2dbe76cddb0c","created_at":"2026-05-29T02:02:14.061932044Z","description":"PROBLEM: When a `ddx try`/`ddx work` attempt fails with any non-success\noutcome (`provider_connectivity`, `no_evidence_produced`, `execution_failed`,\n`structural_validation_failed`, `land_conflict`, `post_run_check_failed`),\nits execute-bead worktree under `~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-\u003cbead\u003e-\u003cattemptID\u003e/`\nis not removed. Cleanup happens only when a *subsequent attempt of the same bead*\nis launched and `RecoverOrphans` runs as pre-spawn sweep.\n\nOBSERVED 2026-05-28 in niflheim drain: a 6.6 GB worktree\n`.execute-bead-wt-niflheim-07f6f8ff-20260528T164209-f179349f` remained 9+\nhours after its attempt failed with `provider_connectivity` (bragi 502).\nThe bead's retry resolved as `already_satisfied` without spawning a new\nexecute-bead worktree (the AC was already met by partially-landed commits\nfrom the failed attempt), so `RecoverOrphans` was never called for that\nbead — the leak is permanent until another attempt at the same bead runs.\n\nThe user reports seeing this pattern consistently across drains, not just\non the one observed bead.\n\nROOT CAUSE: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:393-411`\ndefines `RecoverOrphans` with the docstring \"the parent's responsibility —\ncall it before spawning new workers so stale worktrees from crashed previous\nattempts do not accumulate.\" The function matches strictly by\n`basenamePrefix := ExecuteBeadWtPrefix + beadID + \"-\"` and is only invoked\non a new-attempt pre-spawn path. Two leak conditions follow:\n\n  (a) Bead's retry succeeds via `already_satisfied` (no new worktree\n      spawned because the AC check resolves before agent invocation).\n      The orphan from the prior failed attempt is never matched.\n  (b) Bead is closed for any reason after a failed attempt and is never\n      re-attempted (most common in real flows — failures often resolve\n      via decomposition into different child beads, never returning to\n      the original).\n\nThe post-attempt outcome classifier at\n`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1966` (Classify worker outcome:\ntask_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes / ...) is the natural place\nto call `WorktreeRemove` for non-success classes, but does not.\nThe existing landing path at\n`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1088,1329,1679` calls\n`gitOps.RemoveWorktree` correctly for landing-time temp worktrees, but\nthose are different (per-landing) worktrees, not the per-attempt execute-bead\nworktree under `~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/`.\n\nPROPOSED FIX: Add an attempt-finalize cleanup hook that runs after outcome\nclassification (around `execute_bead.go:1966-2017`) and removes the\nper-attempt execute-bead worktree when the outcome is anything other than\n`task_succeeded`. Preserve only when:\n  1. The operator explicitly opted in via `ddx try --no-merge` (preserve\n     for inspection regardless of outcome).\n  2. An `executions_mirror.go` write or evidence-capture step requires the\n     worktree to persist past classification (verify by reading the call\n     chain at `execute_bead.go:2069-2114`).\n\nImplementation steps the executing agent should follow:\n  1. Read `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1423-2017` to confirm the\n     outcome-classification flow and identify the right post-classify hook\n     point.\n  2. Add a helper `cleanupAttemptWorktree(gitOps, workDir, wtPath, outcome,\n     preserveFlag)` that calls `gitOps.WorktreeRemove(workDir, wtPath)` for\n     non-success outcomes unless `preserveFlag` is set.\n  3. Wire it into the outcome-classification path.\n  4. Add a unit test asserting the cleanup branch for each non-success\n     outcome class, and a negative test confirming success-path keeps the\n     worktree (landing path owns that cleanup).\n  5. Update `RecoverOrphans` docstring to reflect that it's now a\n     belt-and-suspenders sweep, not the primary cleanup path.\n\nNON-SCOPE:\n- Changing `RecoverOrphans` behavior or call sites (keep it; it covers\n  hard-crash cases the new hook can't reach).\n- Adding a periodic background sweeper or `ddx doctor` flag for stale\n  worktrees (potential follow-up).\n- Changing the worktree path under `~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/`.\n- Changing outcome-classification logic or outcome taxonomy.\n- Adding telemetry/metrics for leak rates.\n\nIN-SCOPE FILES:\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (add the cleanup hook + helper)\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_test.go or sibling _test file (add\n  cleanup tests for each non-success outcome)\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go (docstring update on\n  RecoverOrphans only; behavior unchanged)\n\nOUT-OF-SCOPE FILES:\n- execute_bead_land.go — landing-time temp-worktree cleanup is correct\n  and untouched.\n- Any code under cli/cmd/ — no CLI surface change required.\n- docs/ — documentation update is downstream of the spec-evolution bead\n  ddx-828a1b8f (worktree resource isolation) and not required here.","events_attachment":"ddx-274943fc/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-274943fc","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:execution","kind:bug","kind:operability","phase:iterate","spec:FEAT-010"],"needs-human-reason":"consecutive_wedge","needs-human-since":"2026-05-31T05:20:40Z","needs-human-source":"ddx work","needs-human-suggested-action":"investigate the repeated wedge (resolver hang / phase-empty heartbeats) and clear the operator-attention state once resolved","needs-human-summary":"bead wedged on 2 consecutive claims (\u003e= threshold 2; last wedge \"progress_watchdog\"); stopped re-claiming and flagged for operator attention","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-90ab9f5e","spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: clean up worktree on non-success attempt outcomes (currently leaks on failed-then-retried-as-already_satisfied path)","updated_at":"2026-06-06T02:28:44.767198655Z"}

Exported fields include at minimum: ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Mode, IdleInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, SpecVersion. Audit: any field present in cobra extraction (agent_cmd.go:1582-1605) or workers.go:25-49 not in this list is a bug.\n\n2. The unified spec does not contain a PollInterval field, a poll_interval JSON tag, or any public compatibility field for the removed polling flag. New CLI/server/API inputs use Mode plus IdleInterval.\n\n3. parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd *cobra.Command) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions, error) exists in the new package. Called only from cobra RunE in agent_cmd.go. No parallel cobra-extraction block remains. DispatchOptions holds control-plane flags (--local, --json) and is not persisted to spec.json.\n\n4. parseExecuteLoopSpec maps --once to Mode=once, maps --watch to Mode=watch, defaults to Mode=drain, maps --idle-interval to IdleInterval only for Mode=watch, and returns a usage error when --idle-interval is provided without --watch.\n\n5. Spec.Validate() and Spec.ApplyDefaults() methods exist. Lifecycle enforced: (a) client calls Validate() before HTTP submit or inline dispatch; (b) server handleStartExecuteLoopWorker calls ApplyDefaults() then Validate() after decode, before StartExecuteLoop; (c) spec.json on disk is fully resolved (ApplyDefaults already ran at write time).\n\n6. handleStartExecuteLoopWorker (cli/internal/server/server.go:2390) decodes directly into ExecuteLoopSpec; the anonymous request struct (currently lines 2395-2409) is deleted. Mode, IdleInterval, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, and FromRev survive the REST round-trip.\n\n7. graphql_adapters.go anonymous request struct (lines 63-76) and its parallel StartExecuteLoop literal (lines 127-141) are deleted. GraphQL dispatch goes through the same server-side ApplyDefaults + Validate + StartExecuteLoop as the REST handler.\n\n8. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec in cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 is replaced by or aliased to ExecuteLoopSpec. spec.json files written by StartExecuteLoop use the unified struct and emit Mode plus IdleInterval.\n\n9. runWorker (cli/internal/server/workers.go:658) consumes spec.MaxCostUSD as the cost-cap ceiling, with escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD fallback when zero. No hardcoded DefaultMaxCostUSD reference outside the fallback case.\n\n10. OpaquePassthrough: server-submitted spec with OpaquePassthrough=true skips both validation gates - workers.go:357-361 (StartExecuteLoop pre-flight) and the ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService branch inside runWorker. TestStartExecuteLoop_OpaquePassthrough_SkipsValidation asserts that StartExecuteLoop with OpaquePassthrough=true does not call ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService and returns a WorkerRecord without error even when the harness name is unresolvable.\n\n11. Reflection round-trip test: TestExecuteLoopSpec_RoundTripsAllFields_Reflection in cli/internal/agent/spec_test.go or executeloop sub-package. Uses reflect to iterate exported fields of ExecuteLoopSpec; for each field sets a non-zero sentinel value; marshals to JSON; unmarshals; asserts equality. Test fails if any exported field has a zero-value sentinel after unmarshal. A future added field automatically falls under coverage.\n\n12. Backward compatibility for disk only: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ load without error. Files containing unknown legacy fields are tolerated and ignored by json.Unmarshal default behavior. Do not enable json.Decoder.DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads.\n\n13. Forward compatibility: SpecVersion int field present. Server returns HTTP 400 with a capability description when incoming SpecVersion \u003e server-supported version. SpecVersion==0 (unset) is treated as the current version. TestHandleStartExecuteLoopWorker_HighSpecVersion_Returns400 covers this.\n\n14. Regression: inline execution path behavior is unchanged except for the explicit once/drain/watch mapping. When a flag or worker mode is added to cobra, the developer must also update the REST handler request struct in server.go and the GraphQL adapter request struct in graphql_adapters.go; missing one silently drops the field at runtime. The new work-loop contract requires one vocabulary across CLI parsing, worker persistence, REST, and GraphQL: mode plus idle_interval, with no public compatibility alias for the removed polling flag.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nEach work field traverses six independent declaration points with no compile-time linkage:\n\n1. cobra flag extraction plus local vars: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605 extracts fromRev, harness, model, profile, provider, modelRef, effort, once/watch mode, idleInterval, noReview, reviewHarness, reviewModel, maxCostUSD, requestTimeout, minPower, and maxPower as naked locals.\n\n2. positional-param pass-down: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1744-1773 closes over these locals and assembles config.CLIOverrides by hand; no single struct groups them.\n\n3. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec definition: cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 is hand-maintained in parallel with cobra and has historically missed fields.\n\n4. handleStartExecuteLoopWorker request struct: cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409 is a separate anonymous struct, so newly added fields can be silently lost for server-spawned workers.\n\n5. StartExecuteLoop literal: cli/internal/server/server.go:2441-2455 plus cli/internal/server/workers.go:345-361 hand-assembles the worker spec; any field absent in step 4 cannot appear here.\n\n6. GraphQL adapter request struct plus StartExecuteLoop literal: cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-76 plus 127-141 is a third parallel declaration; fixes to REST can leave GraphQL broken.\n\nThe consumer (cli/internal/server/workers.go:658-926 runWorker) reads MaxCostUSD from escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD hardcoded (line ~753) instead of spec.MaxCostUSD because the field was never plumbed through. OpaquePassthrough has two validation skip gates that must both fire (workers.go:357-361 and the ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService branch in runWorker).\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go: define ExecuteLoopSpec with all fields currently spread across the six layers. Fields: ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Mode, IdleInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, SpecVersion int. JSON tags drive both REST wire format and disk persistence.\n- Mode is the canonical once/drain/watch selector. CLI --once maps to Mode=once. CLI --watch maps to Mode=watch. Default is Mode=drain.\n- IdleInterval is meaningful only for Mode=watch. --idle-interval without --watch is a usage error in the cobra layer and must not be represented as a separate polling mode in the persisted spec.\n- Do not add, accept, document, or emit a public compatibility alias for the removed polling flag. Historical worker records may be read best-effort as unknown/legacy input, but new CLI/server/API inputs use Mode plus IdleInterval.\n- Add Spec.Validate() and Spec.ApplyDefaults(). Add parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd *cobra.Command) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions, error) as the single cobra-to-spec conversion.\n- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605: replace naked-local extraction with parseExecuteLoopSpec. Remove positional-param closure pattern; pass spec by value.\n- cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49: replace ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec with (or alias to) ExecuteLoopSpec from the new package. Delete the parallel struct.\n- cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409: delete the anonymous request struct in handleStartExecuteLoopWorker; decode directly into ExecuteLoopSpec. Call ApplyDefaults() then Validate() before StartExecuteLoop.\n- cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-76 plus 127-141: delete the parallel anonymous struct and parallel StartExecuteLoop literal; route through the same server-side ApplyDefaults + Validate + StartExecuteLoop as REST.\n- cli/internal/server/workers.go:~753: replace hardcoded escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD with spec.MaxCostUSD, with DefaultMaxCostUSD as fallback when zero.\n- cli/internal/agent/spec_test.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec_test.go: TestExecuteLoopSpec_RoundTripsAllFields_Reflection uses reflect over exported fields; sets non-zero value per field; marshals; unmarshals; asserts round-trip. New field = automatic coverage.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7) owns replacing runWorker with exec ddx work; this bead does not change the subprocess vs in-process dispatch decision.\n- Other commands (ddx agent run, ddx try, MCP agent dispatch) may have the same smell; covered by sister bead ddx-fb290074.\n- Reshaping LabelFilter from string to []string is owned by ddx-9d55601f; keep LabelFilter as string.\n\nSEQUENCING\nddx-29058e2a (this bead) vs ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7 - server-spawn migration to exec ddx work): lands independently and before ddx-eccc6efb. ADR-022 says ddx-29058e2a remains valuable as worker-side struct unification (cobra to in-process flow) and should land independently. When ddx-eccc6efb lands later, it will delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec from workers.go as part of the exec migration; the unified ExecuteLoopSpec should already live in the durable agent package. If ddx-eccc6efb lands first, the scope of this bead narrows to the cobra and worker-facing layers that still
