agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=8503 cost_usd=0.8610"},{"actor":"erik","body":"Architectural conflict with open bead ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7), plus the bead materially exceeds the size-check thresholds. Human triage required to resolve the design conflict before any unification work commits. ## Summary The bead asks to UNIFY the parallel ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec / cobra-flag / HTTP-request structs into one canonical `ExecuteLoopSpec` struct used at every layer. But the open ADR-022 step 7 epic (ddx-eccc6efb, status=open) takes the opposite direction: DELETE `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` entirely and replace the in-process server-spawn path with `exec ddx work` (subprocess), at which point there are no parallel structs because the server stops marshalling a spec at all — it just builds an argv vector. These are mutually-exclusive architectural directions for the same code. Landing this bead's unification would either: (a) be partially undone when ADR-022 step 7 deletes the unified struct, or (b) lock in the unified-struct design and require ADR-022 step 7 to be re-scoped or abandoned. The bead's \"SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS\" section names dc157075, 9d55601f, and a946c744, but is silent on ddx-eccc6efb / ADR-022 step 7. That gap is the load-bearing decision this bead does not make. ## Evidence 1. `cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49` — current `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` exists with the fields the bead lists (and is missing MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev as the bead claims). 2. ADR-022 step 7 (ddx-eccc6efb) is OPEN with acceptance: \"Legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec serialization deletes when this lands.\" Confirmed via `ddx bead show ddx-eccc6efb`. 3. ADR-022 step 4 (ddx-85165814) already CLOSED → `--local` is a deprecated no-op (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1576-1577`). The cobra path no longer branches to server-submit; `executeLoopWithServer` (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1986`) is unreferenced (verified: only the function definition matches; no call sites). 4. Therefore the bead's USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM (\"ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server\") cannot be reproduced via the CLI today — `--local` is a no-op and the server-submit branch from cobra is dead code. The remaining server-spawn path is via `POST /api/agent/workers/work` (`cli/internal/server/server.go:2390`) and the GraphQL `workerDispatchAdapter` (`cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127`), both of which ADR-022 step 7 wants to delete in favor of subprocess exec. 5. Recent ddx-eccc6efb attempt (20260503T065047-61e4ed20, result.json) was DECLINED for size and decomposed into proposed children 7a/7b/7c — those children may not yet be filed in the main repo per its own bookkeeping note, but the intent stands. ## Size-check verdict (independent of the conflict) The bead also fails the size check on its own: - 17 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems (cobra flag binding, client-side validation, HTTP handler, GraphQL adapter, workers.go consumer, on-disk persistence, backward-compat for legacy fields, forward-compat with SpecVersion, custom Duration codec, reflection-driven test, manual end-to-end run). - Touches at minimum: new package `cli/internal/agent/executeloop/` (struct + Validate + ApplyDefaults + custom Duration + tests), `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` (cobra rebind + dispatch split), `cli/internal/server/server.go` (handler rewrite + handler tests), `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` + `graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go` + `workers_config_test.go` (parallel-construction deletion), `cli/internal/server/workers.go` (consumer rewrite), and a literal sweep across at least 8 test files (`workers_test.go`, `workers_e2e_test.go`, `workers_prune_test.go`, `workers_stop_test.go`, `workers_stop_propagation_test.go`, `workers_watchdog_test.go`, plus the two GraphQL test files) that all construct `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{}` literals. - File-size signal: workers.go 1959 LOC, server.go 5248 LOC, agent_cmd.go 2245 LOC, workers_test.go 1107 LOC. Estimated change footprint comfortably \u003e500 LOC across \u003e5 files in unrelated packages. - Per execution-bead protocol size check: \"More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems\" + \"More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages\" → DECOMPOSE. ## Why I am not decomposing in this attempt The protocol's decomposition path commits the project to a particular shape (in this case: unification rather than deletion). Filing 4–6 child beads under ddx-29058e2a would advance an architectural direction that is in unresolved conflict with ddx-eccc6efb. A clean decomposition only makes sense AFTER the parent-vs-step-7 design choice is made by a human: Option A — Drop ddx-29058e2a in favor of ADR-022 step 7. The parallel-struct smell goes away by deletion, not unification. The 5 currently-dropped flags can be patched as a narrow fix on the existing struct in the meantime if the operator workflow needs them, with a note that step 7 will erase the struct. Option B — Keep ddx-29058e2a and amend / close ddx-eccc6efb. The unified spec becomes the canonical wire format; ADR-022 step 7 reduces in scope (or is closed as superseded) because the server keeps marshalling a spec, just a unified one. Option C — Sequence: land the narrow 5-flag fix now (small bead), then land ADR-022 step 7 (which deletes the struct and obsoletes the parallel-struct smell), then close ddx-29058e2a as superseded. Each option has different downstream implications (ddx-fb290074 audit scope, REACH-PROTO entry roots, where Validate/ApplyDefaults live, whether SpecVersion is needed at all). I do not have authority to pick. ## Recommended next actions for the orchestrator / triage 1. Decide which of A / B / C above is the intended path. This is a 5-minute design call, not a research task. 2. If A: close ddx-29058e2a as superseded; optionally file a small fix bead \"add MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev to ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec + propagate through handler\" (~50 LOC, will be deleted by step 7 anyway). 3. If B: amend ddx-eccc6efb's scope (or close it), then decompose ddx-29058e2a into ~5 children: (i) new spec package + Validate/ApplyDefaults + Duration codec + reflection test, (ii) cobra rebind + parseExecuteLoopSpec + dispatch split, (iii) HTTP handler decode-into-spec rewrite + delete handler-local request struct, (iv) GraphQL adapter unification, (v) workers.go consumer migration + workers_*_test literal sweep + spec.json on-disk format flip + SpecVersion gate. 4. If C: file the narrow 5-flag fix as its own bead now; pause ddx-29058e2a until after step 7 lands, at which point most of its ACs disappear and what's left (if anything) can be re-scoped. ## What this attempt did NOT do (and why) - No code changes. The conflict is upstream of any implementation choice. - No child beads filed. Filing children would commit to the unification path; see \"Why I am not decomposing\" above. - No partial fix to the 5 dropped flags. The bead explicitly absorbs that narrow fix; doing it here without addressing the unification framing would muddy the bead's status.","created_at":"2026-05-03T11:09:21.004532662Z","kind":"no_changes_needs_investigation","source":"ddx agent work","summary":"no_changes_needs_investigation"},{"actor":"erik","body":"no_changes\nrationale: status: needs_investigation\nreason: Architectural conflict with open bead ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7), plus the bead materially exceeds the size-check thresholds. .ddx/beads.jsonl:91:{"acceptance":"1. New test (e.g. TestExecuteBead_TrackerCommitIndexContentionIsTransientNotBeadFailure): simulate the tracker commit at execute_bead.go:2286 hitting \"fatal: unable to write new index file: exit status 128\" (and index.lock) under a held lock; assert the bead is NOT marked failed and the tracker commit retries/succeeds on a subsequent attempt.\n2. The tracker-commit path and the durable-audit-commit path share one transient-contention classifier (no divergent copy); a test asserts both call-sites treat \"unable to write new index file\" as transient.\n3. Regression: a genuine non-transient tracker-commit failure (e.g. permission) still fails the bead as before.\n4. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestExecuteBead_TrackerCommit|TestIsTransientGitContention' -count=1 green.\n5. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... -count=1 green.\n6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"835bfe84b6419108f4422e119671b9ef7ba7d9ab","created_at":"2026-06-06T06:38:05.516516911Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-2965925e","depends_on_id":"ddx-83361480","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-06-06T06:38:06Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM (captured live, 2nd 2-worker collision): With two `ddx work --watch` workers on /Users/erik/Projects/ddx, the DURABLE-AUDIT commit path correctly retried index contention (ddx-82c89a10: \"transient git index/ref contention committing durable audit outputs ... will retry next iteration\" -\u003e merged), BUT a SEPARATE commit path — the tracker commit at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2286 (`return fmt.Errorf(\"committing tracker: %s: %w\", ...)`) — does NOT route through the transient-retry, so the SAME \"fatal: unable to write new index file: exit status 128\" contention FAILED 3 beads outright: ddx-3fd19af1, ddx-122e0ec5, ddx-e6f047cd (\"committing tracker: fatal: unable to write new index file: exit status 128\"). So the resilience added for the durable-audit commit (ddx-23ac2796, and pending ddx-83361480/ddx-e8bf0695) does NOT protect the tracker-commit call-site.\n\nROOT CAUSE: the tracker commit at execute_bead.go:2286 (and its caller) returns the git error directly as a hard failure; it does not classify \"unable to write new index file\" / index.lock / \"signal: killed\" as transient nor retry, unlike the durable-audit path's finalizeDurableAuditOrStop + isTransientGitContention (execute_bead_loop.go:1026,1288). Two workers committing tracker state to the same shared .git/index contend, and the loser fails its bead instead of retrying.\n\nPROPOSED FIX: Route the tracker commit through the SAME transient-contention handling as the durable-audit commit — classify index-write/index.lock/ref-lock/SIGKILL/deadline contention (via the shared isTransientGitContention, extended by ddx-83361480) as transient and retry on a later iteration (the tracker commit should be idempotent — re-deriving and re-committing the same staged state), rather than failing the bead. Prefer a single shared resilient-commit helper used by BOTH the durable-audit path and the execute_bead.go:2286 tracker path so the two cannot diverge again.\n\nNON-SCOPE: the durable-audit classifier/deadline (ddx-83361480, ddx-e8bf0695 — this bead reuses that classifier); worktree/file leaks (ddx-b320e794, ddx-274943fc).","events_attachment":"ddx-2965925e/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-2965925e","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:execution","area:git","kind:bug","kind:reliability","phase:iterate","spec:TP-021","spec:FEAT-010","prevention:ddx-23ac2796","incident:durable-audit-collision"],"parent":"ddx-3106674f","priority":0,"queue-rank":-1,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-d658e1a2","status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: 'committing tracker' commit path lacks transient-retry on index contention — fails beads under 2-worker load","updated_at":"2026-06-07T00:49:36.324157112Z"}

The checked-in sample config also does not document the bead_tracker backend block yet (.ddx/config.yaml:1-17).\n\nROOT CAUSE\ncli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137 and .ddx/config.yaml:1-17 do not present Axon as a normal selectable backend. block.\n- Keep the default backend unchanged when backend is unset.\n- Preserve bd/br behavior for the existing external backend path.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- GraphQL client generation.\n- Axon backend CRUD internals.\n- Subscription .ddx/beads.jsonl:96:{"acceptance":"1. `TestInstall_InTreeWritesProjectTreeAndLinks` (cli/cmd/install_test.go:21) asserts `ddx install \u003cname\u003e` in in-tree mode writes to `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/` and creates project-tier links under `\u003cproject\u003e/.claude/skills/\u003cname\u003e` and `\u003cproject\u003e/.agents/skills/\u003cname\u003e`.\n2. `TestInstall_ConventionModeWritesXDGProjectTree` (cli/cmd/install_test.go:73) asserts `ddx install \u003cname\u003e` in convention mode writes to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/\u003chost\u003e/\u003cowner\u003e/\u003crepo\u003e/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/` and creates project-tier links pointing into that XDG plugins path.\n3. `TestUpdate_RespectsConventionVsInTreeMode` (cli/cmd/update_test.go:247) asserts `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.\n4. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... green.\n5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"8fbb32ad36b842cefed25f048cd5511a8b64afe9","created_at":"2026-06-03T11:21:13.13129759Z","description":"PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a, slice 3 of 4)\n\nWithout `--global`, install and update must target the project's ddx root, which differs by mode: in-tree (`\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e`) versus convention (`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/\u003chost\u003e/\u003cowner\u003e/\u003crepo\u003e/plugins/\u003cname\u003e`). Either way the project-tier agent skill links (`\u003cproject\u003e/.claude/skills` and `\u003cproject\u003e/.agents/skills`) must point into whichever plugins tree is active.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- `resolveInstallDirs(global=false)` (cli/cmd/install.go:184, project branch install.go:195-197) sets `installRoot = ddxroot.Path(ctx, f.WorkingDir)` and skill dirs to `\u003cworkingDir\u003e/.agents/skills` and `\u003cworkingDir\u003e/.claude/skills`. `ddxroot.Path` (cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:75) returns the in-tree `.ddx` path when `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx` exists and the convention `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/...` path otherwise, so mode selection is implicit.\n- `installLocal` non-global branch derives `ddxRootDir := ddxroot.Path(...)` (install.go:453) for the same routing.\n- Update: the non-global path `performUpdate` (cli/cmd/update.go:204) uses `ddxroot.JoinProject(workingDir, ...)` (e.g. update.go:227) so it updates the project install in whichever mode is active; mode is not an explicit flag but follows `ddxroot`.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nEnsure `ddx install \u003cname\u003e` (no `--global`) in in-tree mode writes to `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/` and links `\u003cproject\u003e/.claude/skills/\u003cname\u003e` and `\u003cproject\u003e/.agents/skills/\u003cname\u003e`; in convention mode (no `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/`) writes to the XDG project plugins path and links the project-tier skill dirs into that path. Ensure `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- The `--global` install/update surface (slice 2).\n- `ddx init` behavior and doctor reporting (slice 4).\n- Removing either mode — in-tree and convention coexist.\n\nDEPS\n- Slice 1 (plugin lookup precedence) and ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — project paths route through `ddxroot.Path()`.","events_attachment":"ddx-2b9c26d7/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-2b9c26d7","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:plugin","area:cli","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas","parent:ddx-602ae31a","dep:ddx-06cbaa90"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-c7bae1d6","status":"closed","title":"project install surface: ddx install (no flag) and ddx update honor in-tree vs convention mode","updated_at":"2026-06-03T12:16:40.973821743Z","work-no-changes-count":1}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:97:{"acceptance":"1. TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal (cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go:23) asserts that when both the project and global trees contain the plugin, ResolvePlugin returns the project path and layer \"project\".\n2. TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal (cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go:52) asserts that when the project copy is absent, ResolvePlugin returns the ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e path and layer \"global\".\n3. A test asserts ResolvePlugin returns (\"\", \"baked-in\", nil) for name == \"ddx\" when neither project nor global copy exists, and a non-nil error for any other unresolved name.\n4. The skill-install-topology ADR and the CLAUDE.md \"Persona System\" precedence note are landed in this bead's commit.\n5. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/registry/... green.\n6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"c4d239774d5fd3aac03fe55d567cc86292147d8a","created_at":"2026-06-03T01:02:41.180499807Z","description":"PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)\n\nSkills/personas/plugins can live in two install layers (project-scoped under ddxroot.Path() and machine-wide under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/). Runtime callers need a single authoritative resolver that picks the right on-disk plugin directory with a defined precedence, otherwise the dual-location install surface has no consumer and project installs cannot shadow global ones.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:22 ResolvePlugin must implement precedence project → global → baked-in. The project path is filepath.Join(ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot), \"plugins\", name) (resolve.go:23); the global path is filepath.Join(ddxroot.GlobalDir(), \"plugins\", name) (resolve.go:28); baked-in (defaultplugin.FS()) is valid only for name == \"ddx\" (resolve.go:33). ddxroot.GlobalDir() is defined at cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:90.\n- Before this layer existed, install/update targeted ddxroot.Path() unconditionally and there was no precedence rule, so an operator with both a project and a global copy had undefined resolution.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name) returns (path, layer, err): when both project and global copies exist, return the project path with layer \"project\"; when only the global copy exists, return its path with layer \"global\"; when neither exists and name == \"ddx\", return (\"\", \"baked-in\", nil) so callers fall back to defaultplugin.FS(); otherwise return a non-nil error naming all three layers.\n2. The project path resolves through ddxroot.Path() so it is correct in BOTH in-tree mode (\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e) and convention mode (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/\u003cidentity\u003e/plugins/\u003cname\u003e).\n3. SPEC IMPACT: file/update the skill-install-topology ADR (docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-025-or-equivalent) documenting the three-layer precedence, and update the CLAUDE.md \"Persona System\" section to state the project-over-global-over-baked-in lookup precedence.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Does NOT remove the per-project install path (both layers coexist).\n- Does NOT migrate any legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.\n- Does NOT add agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.\n- Writing the plugin content / creating agent-tier links is handled by the install and init child beads, not here.\n\nDEPS\nddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — project path routes through ddxroot.Path() and the global path through ddxroot.GlobalDir(); both must be agreed-on. Governing epic: ddx-602ae31a (Principle 2: skills/personas install-to-agent vs install-in-project).","events_attachment":"ddx-2bb4e0a5/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-2bb4e0a5","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:registry","area:plugin","area:config","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-ce0e5820","status":"closed","title":"registry: plugin lookup precedence resolver (project → global → baked-in) + topology spec","updated_at":"2026-06-03T01:07:29.360514627Z"}

The test uses static source inspection or injected `recordingGitOps`; it does not create a network remote or call `git fetch` against a hosted origin.\n4. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'TestWorkerPathDoesNotUseFetchOriginAncestryCheck|NetworkFree|Fetch'` passes.\n5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"3beb3315db81a6303160b566badd689dc535b7b9","created_at":"2026-06-05T02:54:00.11949555Z","description":"PROBLEM\nThe worker path is intended to be network-free, but the fetch-based ancestry checker remains available and holds the main-git lock while running `git fetch`. .ddx/beads.jsonl:102:{"acceptance":"1. TestReadinessChecksSchema in cli/internal/agent/readiness_schema_test.go validates every verdict form the decoder accepts (bool true, bool false, string, null, absent) against cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json AND asserts an object verdict is rejected, locking producer/consumer alignment.\n2. The producer prompt buildPreClaimIntakePrompt (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:636-655) references the canonical schema path via the readinessChecksSchemaPath constant (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:21), not a duplicated string literal.\n3. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... green\n4. lefthook run pre-commit passes","closing_commit_sha":"rev-ddx-2e3ad63d","created_at":"2026-05-28T09:08:21.2215798Z","description":"PROBLEM\nThe pre_claim_intake readiness payload schema can drift between the producer prompt and the Go decoder. That drift is exactly the class of bug that wedged the fizeau queue: the producer emitted readiness_checks[].verdict as a JSON bool while the consumer parsed a string, so every claim attempt failed with a warn-only rejection and the queue idled. There must be a single shared schema that both sides consume, plus a CI-run test that fails the moment the schema and decoder diverge.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- The producer prompt references the schema only by a path string in prose: cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:639 (buildPreClaimIntakePrompt) writes \"Canonical schema: \"+readinessChecksSchemaPath, and the constant lives at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:21. Because the prompt text and the schema file are independent, instructions can drift from the schema with nothing catching it.\n- The consumer decoder accepts bool/string/null/absent verdict forms: readinessVerdict.UnmarshalJSON at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:97-125 and preClaimReadinessChecksPayload.UnmarshalJSON at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:386-416.\n- The schema verdict union is cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json:44-50 (oneOf boolean/string/null). Nothing fails CI if someone tightens the schema, or changes the decoder, so that the two diverge.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nKeep cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json as the single source of truth consumed by both the producer prompt (via the readinessChecksSchemaPath constant) and the consumer decoder. Ensure a CI-run test validates every verdict form the decoder accepts against the schema and asserts that an object verdict is rejected, so any tightening of the schema or change to the decoder that breaks alignment fails the suite. Confirm the producer prompt references the canonical path constant rather than a duplicated literal.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Do NOT implement the runtime same-fingerprint warn escalation guard (owned by the sibling child bead).\n- Do NOT change the verdict coercion semantics (bool true-\u003epass, bool false-\u003efail, string-\u003elowercased, null/absent-\u003eempty).\n- Do NOT introduce go.mod replace directives.\n\nDEPENDENCIES: no deps.","events_attachment":"ddx-2e3ad63d/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-2e3ad63d","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:execute-bead","area:pre-claim","kind:reliability","observability","priority:reliability","decomposed","needs-human-decomposition","triage:no-changes-unjustified"],"parent":"ddx-fd02cf6e","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"Lock readiness_checks schema as the shared producer/consumer source of truth with a CI drift gate","updated_at":"2026-05-28T10:31:56.276408092Z","work-no-changes-count":1}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:108:{"acceptance":"1. The docker-clone backend (cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:293) configures process-group and/or container teardown so its nested harness is reaped on worker exit, including abnormal exit. TestDockerCloneBackend_ProcessGroupCleanup asserts the docker command is started with cmdSetProcessGroup applied (Setpgid set) and/or that container teardown runs on all exit paths.\n2. If container teardown already guarantees reaping without process-group handling, the test instead documents and asserts the unconditional teardown path (e.g. --rm plus an always-run dockerRemoveContainer/`docker stop`), with the justifying code lines cited.\n3. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... green.\n4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"7dc4d993a633b2c04c856952941a078ca98d8cdb","created_at":"2026-05-27T08:12:54.936086647Z","description":"PROBLEM\nThe docker-clone attempt backend spawns the per-attempt `docker` invocation WITHOUT a process group, so even graceful worker cleanup may not reach the harness running nested inside the container. On abnormal or even graceful worker exit, the container (and the harness it hosts) can be left running, mirroring the PID-1 orphan leak seen for the worktree/local-clone backends. (worktree/local-clone backends route through cli/internal/agent/executor.go:156-157 and DO get Setpgid via cmdSetProcessGroup; the docker path does not.)\n\nROOT CAUSE (file:line)\n- cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:293 — DockerClone backend builds `cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, \"docker\", args...)` with NO call to cmdSetProcessGroup, unlike the executor spawn path. The only teardown is dockerRemoveContainer at attempt_backend.go:299-301, gated on ctx.Err() != nil, which does not run when the parent dies abnormally.\n- cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:120-122 (worktree Run) and :182-184 (local-clone Run) dispatch through the executor and inherit Setpgid; the docker backend bypasses that path.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nMake the docker-clone backend reap its nested harness on worker exit: apply cmdSetProcessGroup to the docker invocation at attempt_backend.go:293, and/or run the container with a teardown guarantee (run with --rm and ensure dockerRemoveContainer / `docker stop` runs on ALL exit paths, not only ctx.Err() != nil) so the container tree is torn down when the worker exits. If existing container teardown (e.g. --rm + an unconditional stop on cleanup) already guarantees reaping, document that justification with the specific code lines instead of adding redundant process-group handling.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- The Pdeathsig change in cmdSetProcessGroup and the startup orphan reaper are separate children.\n- Graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown of the worktree/local-clone executor path (executor.go:231-255) is out of scope.\n- The harness-runner-INTERNAL leak is covered by fizeau-50b6f055.\n\nCROSS-REF: fizeau-50b6f055.","events_attachment":"ddx-3113d524/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-3113d524","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:execute-bead","area:workers","kind:bug","kind:reliability","priority:reliability","severity:high","ref:fizeau-50b6f055"],"parent":"ddx-93d2ac52","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-0ff8bc73","status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: give the docker-clone attempt backend process-group/container cleanup so its nested harness is reaped on worker exit","updated_at":"2026-05-27T09:37:55.792616794Z"}

Conventional commit ending [\u003cthis-bead-id\u003e].\n11. lefthook run pre-commit passes.\n\nDEPS (gate execution; bead stays open until these close): refactor children C4-C13 (ddx-358f2457, ddx-c8f79963, ddx-b669bb9f, ddx-06eb05d8, ddx-848069a3, ddx-9228a484, ddx-0b8780db, ddx-c670ef0a, ddx-387a0178).","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:41.880567652Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-358f2457","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-c8f79963","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-b669bb9f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-06eb05d8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-848069a3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-0b8780db","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-c670ef0a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-387a0178","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-7e1fed08","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-07T16:13:04Z"}],"description":"Hand-curated sub-agent prompts have been outperforming ddx work / ddx try in this session. Two competing hypotheses for why:\nH1: ddx execution machinery (the 9 fail-prone layers) is unreliable — failure is in the workers/server code.\nH2: Bead authoring quality is insufficient — beads punt investigation; hand-curation papers over the gap.\n\nBoth are plausible. Determining the relative contribution requires a 3-path study, NOT the original 2-path proposal.\n\nTHREE PATHS\n\nPath A: ddx try / ddx work with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (current production path).\nPath B: Sub-agent dispatched with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (NOT hand-curated). Same prompt as A; different executor. Isolates ddx machinery from prompt quality.\nPath C: Sub-agent dispatched with operator hand-curated prompt (file:line cites, named tests, explicit non-scope, etc.). Establishes the ceiling.\n\nINTERPRETATION\n- A vs B differ → ddx machinery is the failure (file follow-ups against the 9 layers).\n- B vs C differ → bead authoring quality is the failure (already covered by bead-quality-audit + retrofit).\n- A == B \u003c\u003c C → both are real, with bead quality being the dominant gap.\n\nMETHOD\nPick 5 representative CLOSED beads as fixtures: one trivial fix, one refactor child, one test addition, one multi-file feature, one documentation update. For each fixture, run all 3 paths on a branch from the bead's base-rev (parent of the original work commit). Throwaway commits — the original work is already in main. We're measuring path properties, not producing mergeable code.\n\nMEASUREMENTS\nPer path per bead:\n- Time-to-first-claim (A) / time-to-first-output (B,C)\n- Time-to-merge-eligible-commit (whether or not we merge)\n- Attempts (A) / iterations (B,C)\n- Status outcomes: success / no_changes / preserved / blocked / errored\n- Cost: tokens, USD\n- Manual interventions\n- Failure mode classified per the 9-layer machinery list\n\nOUTPUT\n.ddx/executions/\u003crun-id\u003e/ddx-vs-subagent-comparison-2026-05-03.md with:\n- Per-bead per-path table\n- Aggregate stats (median + p99)\n- Failure-mode distribution\n- Hypothesis verdict (H1 vs H2 vs both)\n- Recommendations for which path is appropriate for which bead-shape\n\nTIMING\nThis study runs AFTER the executeloop refactor (C0-C13) lands so the ddx machinery is its post-refactor shape. Running pre-refactor measures behavior we're about to change. RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES + bead-quality-audit + retrofit + refactor must all complete first. This bead is filed now and EXECUTION DEFERRED.\n\nNOT IN SCOPE\n- Fixing whatever the study reveals (separate bug beads filed from the study's findings)\n- Running against pre-refactor ddx machinery (data we won't act on)\n- Comparing different sub-agent harnesses (different concern)\n\nEVIDENCE\n- ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and is partial Path B-vs-C data favoring the bead-quality hypothesis.","id":"ddx-31f745cd","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:agent","area:tooling","bead-quality","kind:study","phase:2","reliability"],"notes":"Partial Path B-vs-C evidence: ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome, supporting the bead-quality hypothesis side of the study.","parent":"ddx-e34994e2","priority":2,"schema_version":1,"status":"open","title":"study: 3-path execution comparison (ddx work vs sub-agent + verbatim bead vs sub-agent + hand-curated)","updated_at":"2026-05-07T16:13:04.858647865Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:111:{"acceptance":"1. TestExecuteBead_IndexLockNotHeldAcrossSubprocess asserts that during an execute-bead run with a mocked long-running harness subprocess, .git/index.lock does not exist (or its mtime is older than the subprocess start) for the duration of the subprocess wait.\n2. TestExecuteBead_TrackerLockNotHeldAcrossSubprocess asserts the same property for .ddx/.git-tracker.lock.\n3. TestExecuteBead_LocksReacquiredForMutation asserts the locks ARE acquired (briefly) when the post-subprocess git stage/commit and tracker update execute.\n4. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/... green.\n5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"b7a87b5742acfdaaf847a6fc6d45d8955077f4b7","created_at":"2026-05-21T12:07:56.888681246Z","description":"PROBLEM\nddx work --watch holds .git/index.lock and .ddx/.git-tracker.lock for the entire execute-bead lifetime, including the multi-minute LLM subprocess wait. Observed 2026-05-17: PID 90753 held .git/index.lock for 6+ minutes while a claude subprocess ran, blocking concurrent bead create/update/close operations with 'tracker lock timeout (max elapsed)' errors.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nThe execute-bead path in cli/cmd/work.go and the tracker mutation path in cli/internal/bead/ acquire the git index lock / .git-tracker.lock once at the start of a worker iteration and release only at the end, spanning the subprocess.Wait() on the agent harness. Search for lock acquisition sites in cli/cmd/work.go (worker loop), cli/cmd/run.go (run harness invocation), and cli/internal/bead/store.go (tracker write path) — the os.Open/flock or LockFile call wrapping the harness invocation is the offender. Executing agent must grep for index.lock / git-tracker.lock acquisition and confirm exact file:line.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nRefactor so each lock is acquired immediately before its corresponding git/tracker mutation and released immediately after. The LLM subprocess invocation (claude/codex/gemini exec.Cmd.Run or .Wait) must run with no DDx-held mutation lock. Stage-and-commit operations should batch mutations to minimize re-acquire churn.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Metric emission (separate child)\n- Hold-time cap enforcement (separate child)\n- Multi-worker concurrency test (separate child)\n- Documentation (separate child)\n- Changes to lock implementation itself (flock vs file-based); only acquisition scope is in scope here.\n\nCROSS-REF\nParent observation from Fizeau alignment-review 2026-05-17 (AR-2026-05-17-repo).","events_attachment":"ddx-329a08b8/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-329a08b8","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:execute-bead","area:workers","kind:bug","kind:reliability","priority:reliability"],"parent":"ddx-57c40485","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-d71e1fb7","status":"closed","title":"scope git index.lock and tracker.lock to mutation window, not LLM subprocess lifetime","updated_at":"2026-05-26T21:45:12.304857716Z"}

The fixtures must be self-contained and must not reference Niflheim paths as required inputs.\n2. `TestExecuteBeadWorker_NiflheimEvidence_EmptyReviewResultCannotClose` proves a non-skipped success path with `cycle_trace.review_result={}` or an equivalent empty `ReviewResult` cannot close a bead and records `review_error` or operator-attention with a non-empty reason.\n3. `TestExecuteBeadWorker_NiflheimEvidence_NilReviewerRequiresExplicitSkip` updates the current nil-reviewer behavior so `Reviewer: nil` only closes when an explicit durable no-review/skip reason is present; otherwise it fails review gating.\n4. `TestExecuteBeadLoop_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationFailureDoesNotRerunImplementation` simulates both observed details (`cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate/merge is already landed, and `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s`) and asserts the action is reconcile/retry-land/operator-attention, not a fresh implementation run and not model escalation.\n5. `TestExecuteBeadLoop_NiflheimEvidence_DecomposedParentIsExecutionIneligible` simulates `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` with `orchestrator_action: decompose`, child bead IDs, and `outcome_reason=decomposed`; it asserts the parent is marked execution-ineligible/proposed or dependency-waiting according to policy and that no further implementation attempt or min-power escalation is scheduled.\n6. `TestWorkRetryEscalation_NiflheimEvidence_OnlySemanticFailuresRaiseMinPower` proves only semantic acceptance/review failures can increase requested min power/profile. Strong-model escalation exists, but it is not reliably tied to the class of failure. The worker spent repeated attempts on structural or system failures that stronger models cannot fix, while automatic review evidence was empty on attempts that were recorded as successful. This violates the DDx engineering principle \"Cheapest Viable, Escalate on Evidence\" (docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:39-45): review, runtime, or retry evidence should determine escalation, and only lower-power inability to meet acceptance criteria should push to stronger routes. It also threatens FEAT-010 task-execution reliability because beads can be retried, escalated, or closed without a typed, auditable reason.\n\nThis bead is a Niflheim incident/regression closure bead. It is not a duplicate of the narrower queue items listed under INTERSECTIONS; instead it captures the external evidence DDx workers will not otherwise have, and requires a DDx-side regression suite proving those observed scenarios classify correctly before rerun, escalation, operator attention, or close.\n\nBACKGROUND AND OBSERVED NIFLHEIM EVIDENCE\nThe evidence was gathered from Niflheim worker logs and result bundles, but those files are not load-bearing for this bead. The relevant facts are copied here because an isolated DDx worker cannot access the Niflheim repository or `/tmp` logs.\n\n1. Decomposed parent kept consuming attempts and escalated to a stronger model anyway.\n- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-64217cc5`, title \"Instrument cold replicator with structured per-operation S3 tracing\".\n- Observed result bundle: `.ddx/executions/20260606T152006-a4ca7254/result.json` plus worker logs from 2026-06-05/2026-06-06.\n- Routing showed escalation exists: repeated routes started at `gpt-5.4-mini` with requested min powers 5, 7, 8, and 9, then reached `gpt-5.5` at requested min power 10.\n- Metrics on the bead showed `attempt_count=24`, `total_tokens=19371353`, and average duration about `244112ms`.\n- Despite triage recommending `close_decomposed_or_mark_execution_ineligible`, retries continued until the latest event recorded:\n  `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale`\n  `powerClass=standard`\n  `rationale: status: open`\n  `orchestrator_action: decompose`\n  `reason: parent bead exceeds the Step 0 size threshold and is already decomposed into focused child beads for tracing, counters, tests, and runbook work.`\n  `child_beads: niflheim-f03c61a2, niflheim-df407aa8, niflheim-7a421ede, niflheim-0bbed6c9`\n  `outcome_reason=decomposed`\n- Expected DDx behavior: once the worker has reliable evidence that the bead is a decomposed parent or execution-ineligible parent, no further implementation attempt or model escalation should occur. It should mark execution-ineligible/proposed/operator-attention according to policy and move on.\n\n2. Land/evidence coordination failures were treated like implementation failure and retried expensively.\n- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-c75aba30`, title \"Materializer per-merge memory footprint OOMs even at concurrent_limit=8\".\n- Two expensive attempts both used `gpt-5.4-mini`; no useful stronger-model escalation was observed.\n- Metrics showed `attempt_count=2`, `total_tokens=65513510`, and average duration about `3306461.5ms`.\n- First attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T113550-7b29ee42/result.json`.\n  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.\n  - Implementation result commit was `7aaeb0712...`; a merge commit `2c6f4d6e6` was later observed as an ancestor of HEAD, but the result still recorded a land coordination failure.\n  - Error detail:\n    `land coordination failed: land advancer: restoring refs/heads/main to ba2d3059f14e834dcb147f2a6db3b294d83b0fac after evidence commit failed: git update-ref refs/heads/main: fatal: update_ref failed for ref 'refs/heads/main': cannot lock ref 'refs/heads/main': is at 5a176b332b182f2a959614f63769d835c47ffd97 but expected 2c6f4d6e6961b8a93742cde66bceec056934655a`\n  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision=\"success\"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.\n- Second attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T142223-94f8f0f8/result.json`.\n  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.\n  - Error detail:\n    `land coordination failed: land advancer: landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s: A .ddx/executions/20260606T150832-af40450b/result.json`\n  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision=\"success\"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.\n- At observation time the worker had claimed it again (`20260606T152024-a21d8c54`) and edited six files in a fresh isolated worktree:\n  `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/mod.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/tests.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/types.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/config.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/executor.rs`, and `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/tests.rs`.\n- Expected DDx behavior: land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures are system or coordination failures, not model-quality failures. The retry policy should reconcile a successfully landed candidate when possible, retry only the land/evidence step when safe, or park/operator-attention the bead when the landing worktree is dirty. It should not blindly rerun implementation at the same or stronger route.\n\n3. Pre-claim intake/system dirt caused repeated warning churn instead of a clear operator lane.\n- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-a62d31ce`, title \"Publish competitive benchmark artifacts and provenance metadata\".\n- A live worker repeatedly ran readiness/intake. The log showed readiness timeout after five minutes, then:\n  `preserved pre-dispatch implementation changes in /home/erik/Projects/niflheim under refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618; released niflheim-a62d31ce and continuing watch. dirty paths: .ddx/harness-sessions/-2929681.json. recover with git stash apply refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618`\n- Bead events included `pre_claim_intake.warn` with timeout/system-unready reason and generic suggested action.\n- Expected DDx behavior: repeated same/systemic pre-claim warnings and dirty generated DDx artifacts should become an operator-visible/system-unready state after the configured threshold, not an indefinite poll/release loop.\n\n4. Automatic reviews were not forcing meaningful quality improvement.\n- Recent Niflheim \"success\" attempts had empty review results:\n  - `niflheim-9dea95da`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T043655-845c77ee/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision=\"success\"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `78e51a168...`.\n  - `niflheim-da4e1311`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T085358-d80ada3e/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision=\"success\"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `d43be3a3...`.\n  - `niflheim-c75aba30` attempts listed above: `cycle_trace.final_decision=\"success\"` with `review_result={}` despite overall land failures.\n- Manual Claude review earlier improved bead wording and completeness, but the automatic worker path did not show enforceable per-AC review evidence.\n- Expected DDx behavior: unless review is explicitly skipped with a durable reason (`runtime.NoReview` or a valid `review:skip` + reason policy), an empty review result must be a review error or operator-attention signal. It must not be treated as approval, success, or evidence of quality improvement.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE\n- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` contains the normative routing table: only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` can escalate; dirty worktree, transport, quota, auth, setup, timeout, and merge-conflict do not escalate. The live Niflheim behavior shows execute-bead/work outcomes are not consistently reduced to this typed table before retry/escalation decisions.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:305-309` wraps `LandingAdvancer` errors as generic `land advancer` errors. The caller can record an overall `execution_failed` without distinguishing landed-candidate reconciliation, ref/index contention, staged evidence dirt, or dirty landing worktree from implementation failure.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1967-2072` emits pre-claim timeout/system-unready outcomes as warn-only by default and continues implementation when strict intake blocking is off. This permits repeated same-fingerprint/system warnings to look like a live worker instead of a system-unready/operator lane.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2175-2315` handles `PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed`, but missing/empty decomposition paths can warn and continue. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3826-3831` can recognize execution-ineligible/decomposed state only after the bead has the right metadata. The Niflheim parent evidence shows structural/decomposed-parent outcomes can still reach repeated implementation attempts and power escalation.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3042-3138` treats success disposition/status as closeable once candidate-cycle review/landing has allowed success, and the old post-merge review is optional. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go:799-823` explicitly proves a worker with `Reviewer: nil` can skip review and close the bead. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:168-193` defines `ReviewResult` with all fields optional except the zero-value enum field; no invariant rejects `{}` as a success-path review artifact.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Add compact Niflheim-style regression fixtures under `cli/internal/agent/testdata/niflheim_worker_evidence/` or equivalent. The fixtures should encode the four scenario families above: empty review success, land coordination/ref-lock/staged-evidence failure, decomposed-parent no-changes rationale with child beads, and repeated pre-claim system-unready warning. Do not depend on Niflheim checkout paths.\n- Introduce or tighten a single execute-bead retry classification boundary that maps attempt result, land result, review result, no-changes rationale, and pre-claim intake outcome into exactly one action: close, retry same tier, escalate min power/profile, reconcile land/evidence, execution-ineligible/proposed, or operator-attention. Wire this boundary into `ddx work`/`ddx try` so `failclass.Classify` is not merely a standalone helper.\n- Enforce review evidence on success. For non-skipped success paths, `cycle_trace.review_result` or the equivalent result field must contain a non-empty verdict, per-AC evidence or documented reason, reviewer routing/session metadata when available, and a classification. `{}` is invalid and must prevent close or produce a review-error/operator-attention event. Explicit review skips remain allowed only when they are durable, reasoned, and visible in result JSON.\n- Classify land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures separately from implementation/model failures. If the candidate commit or merge is already landed or is an ancestor of the target, reconcile and close/continue without rerunning implementation. If the landing worktree has staged generated evidence or DDx metadata, retry/preserve the land/evidence step or move to operator attention according to the existing local reliability policy; do not escalate the model for this class.\n- Classify decomposed-parent/no-changes rationale as execution-ineligible or operator work before another implementation attempt. Stronger models cannot fix parent beads that are already split into executable children.\n- Make worker result JSON and event bodies carry enough audit fields to explain the decision: `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual power/profile/model, review status/verdict, and land/reconcile status.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names. DDx should continue to pass power/profile intent and let Fizeau choose concrete routes.\n- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.\n- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`; reuse or align with it.\n- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`; this bead requires typed classification and review evidence, not another timeout implementation.\n- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`; this bead uses the Niflheim observation as cross-scenario regression coverage.\n- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond the state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.\n\nINTERSECTIONS\n- Parent: `ddx-3106674f` (multi-worker hardening and local reliability gates). This bead is the external Niflheim regression/incident closure layer under that reliability epic.\n- Dependency: `ddx-2965925e` covers tracker commit index contention. This bead must not reimplement that fix; it must prove land/ref/index/evidence failures classify separately from model failure once the low-level path is hardened.\n- Dependency: `ddx-b2846610` covers bounded post-commit review/land. This bead must still require typed review/land outcomes after the timeout wedge is fixed.\n- Dependency: `ddx-d67f124e` covers same-fingerprint pre-claim warning escalation. This bead adds the Niflheim `niflheim-a62d31ce` family as regression evidence.\n- Related but not a dependency: `ddx-e1a576a7` is an execution-ineligible decomposed parent and a local precedent for the `niflheim-64217cc5` class.\n- Related quality framework: `ddx-a9d130d0` and `ddx-ac10e7af` own broader bead lifecycle/operator-lane policy; this bead is concrete worker behavior and evidence gating.","id":"ddx-334c0958","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:iterate","area:agent","area:execute-bead","area:workers","area:review","kind:bug","kind:reliability","severity:critical","spec:FEAT-010","spec:TP-021","adr:024","incident:niflheim-2026-06-06","prevention"],"parent":"ddx-3106674f","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"open","title":"execute-bead worker: gate success on review evidence and classify Niflheim retry failures","updated_at":"2026-06-06T15:55:55.96445459Z"}

Existing post-land failure preservation tests still pass, including `TestLand_PostLandCommandFailurePreserves` or the current equivalent in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go`.\n3. This violates the TP-021 lock-lifetime contract: arbitrary commands must not execute while the main-git lock is held.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1208-1214 wraps all of `landLocked` in `withMainGitLock`.\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1291 and :1369 call `preserveIfPostLandGateFails` inside that locked flow.\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1445-1457 runs `PostLandCommand` with a 30 minute timeout.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Refactor landing so post-land commands run after the main-git mutation lock is released, while preserving existing land/preserve semantics and evidence rewriting.\n- If a post-land command fails, reacquire the lock only for the short preserve/ref/index mutations needed to record that failure.\n- Add a channel-blocking post-land test proving another goroutine can acquire the main-git lock while the command is blocked.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Do not remove post-land command support.\n- Do not change the 30 minute post-land command timeout unless a separate spec updates it.","events_attachment":"ddx-33530e68/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-33530e68","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:iterate","area:agent","kind:bug","spec:TP-021","reliability","concurrency"],"parent":"ddx-3106674f","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-69be6d6b","spec-id":"TP-021","status":"closed","title":"agent: run post-land commands outside the main-git lock","updated_at":"2026-06-06T04:04:17.557075265Z"}

Both tests use an isolated temp HOME/XDG fixture and do not touch the real user home.\n4. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/registry/... green.\n5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"13eaa6a7758297f2001118d4533a33c70a272e9d","created_at":"2026-06-03T17:21:03.026972073Z","description":"PROBLEM (Principle 5 of epic ddx-602ae31a)\nPlugin content can live in two tiers — per-project (ddxroot.Path()/plugins/\u003cname\u003e) and machine-wide global (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e). Runtime resolution must deterministically prefer the project copy when present, fall back to the global copy, and finally to the baked-in default (ddx plugin only). Without a single authoritative resolver, install/update/init/doctor each reinvent path logic and drift.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- internal/registry/resolve.go:29 `ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name)` is the precedence resolver: project path computed at resolve.go:30, global at resolve.go:35, baked-in fallback at resolve.go:44. It must be the only place precedence is decided and must be covered by named tests so install/init/doctor can rely on it.\n- internal/registry/resolve.go:15 `GlobalPluginDir(name)` returns ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e via ddxroot.GlobalDir(); the project path routes through ddxroot.Path() (in-tree vs convention mode handled there).\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. Do not alter install/update/init/doctor call sites here beyond confirming they call `ResolvePlugin` (those are covered by sibling children).\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Removing the per-project install tier (both tiers coexist).\n- Migrating ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.\n- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.\n- install/update/init/doctor wiring (sibling children).\n\nDEPS\nddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — ResolvePlugin routes through both ddxroot.Path() and ddxroot.GlobalDir(); both must be agreed-on before this lands.\n\nGOVERNING ARTIFACTS\n- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md (precedence decision record).\n- CLAUDE.md \"Persona System\" three-layer precedence note.","events_attachment":"ddx-3478153c/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-3478153c","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:config","area:plugin","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-e6291b32","status":"closed","title":"registry: runtime plugin lookup precedence (project → global → baked-in)","updated_at":"2026-06-03T17:23:53.361549509Z","work-no-changes-count":1}

Both tests use an isolated temp HOME/XDG fixture and do not touch the real user home.\n4. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... green.\n5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"32517f0b482ceb9626d9e424c38efc2619ff6dcc","created_at":"2026-06-03T17:21:03.138573805Z","description":"PROBLEM (Principle 3 of epic ddx-602ae31a)\n`ddx init --global` is the umbrella first-time machine setup: it installs the bundled default ddx plugin globally, links the skill into the agent-tier paths so every harness on the machine discovers it, and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults. Confirm `ddx init --global` produces ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx pointing into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/, plus ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml.\n2. Confirm `ddx init` (no flag) creates \u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/ but writes nothing under any plugins/ directory.\n3. New TestExecuteBeadLoop_RepeatedDirtyRootEscalatesWithCooldown (execute_bead_loop_dirty_root_test.go): feed an unchanged dirty path-set across N iterations; assert the Nth exit carries an escalated reason and a cooldown/backoff field rather than an instant re-exit.\n2. Existing execute_bead_loop_dirty_root_test.go and consecutive_wedge_guard_test.go tests pass.\n3. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestExecuteBeadLoop_RepeatedDirtyRootEscalatesWithCooldown|ConsecutiveWedge|DirtyRoot' -count=1 green.\n4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-06-06T21:16:40.77432195Z","description":"PROBLEM: When the main checkout has uncommitted tracked changes, the worker exits operator-attention IMMEDIATELY (attempts:0) and an external supervisor relaunches it, which re-checks, finds the same dirty tree, and instant-exits again — observed ~12x tight loop (resource waste + monitor flood) during the source-leak wedge.\n\nROOT CAUSE: the dirty-root guard at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1531-1554 returns immediately with OperatorAttentionStop{Reason:\"dirty_project_root\"} and setExit(\"OperatorAttention\",...) with NO cooldown or repeat counter. The existing consecutive_wedge_guard (consecutive_wedge_guard_test.go) is not applied to this path.\n\nPROPOSED FIX: Track consecutive dirty_project_root operator-attention exits with the identical dirty path-set; after N (small default, e.g. 3) emit an escalated stop reason and impose a backoff/cooldown (mirroring work-retry-after) so a supervisor relaunch does not tight-spin. Do NOT change the guard's correctness (still halt on a genuinely dirty root); only break the instant-exit relaunch loop. (Source-leak prevention is L1; this is defense-in-depth on the loop side.)\n\nNON-SCOPE: source-leak prevention (L1); the binary-asset build-leak / clean-tree tolerance (ddx-b320e794 — kept separate).","id":"ddx-3499a7ec","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:workers","kind:bug","kind:reliability","phase:iterate","spec:FEAT-010","incident:source-leak-wedge"],"parent":"ddx-3106674f","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"status":"open","title":"agent: escalate (cooldown/backoff) repeated dirty-project-root operator-attention exits instead of instant-exit spin","updated_at":"2026-06-06T21:16:40.77432195Z"}

string.\\\\n    pub fn issue(\\u0026self, claims: \\u0026JwtClaims) -\\u003e Result\\u003cString, AuthError\\u003e {\\\\n        encode(\\\\n            \\u0026Header::new(Algorithm::HS256),\\\\n Format\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\\\\n\\\\nLonger description of what changed and why. data.text: I found the likely surface area in `cli/cmd` and the bead backend; next I’m narrowing to the exact create/add handlers and the existing dependency tests so I can extend the guard without broadening scope.

data.output:
package bead

import (
	"testing"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestSentinelErrors_AreErrorValues(t *testing.T) {
	t.Parallel()

	errs := []error{
		ErrNotFound,
		ErrConflict,
		ErrInvalidID,
		ErrAlreadyClaimed,
		ErrNotClaimedByOwner,
		ErrUnsupported,
	}

	seen := make(map[error]struct{}, len(errs))
	for _, err := range errs {
		require.Error(t, err)
		require.NotEmpty(t, err.Error())
		seen[err] = struct{}{}
	}

	require.Len(t, seen, len(errs), "sentinel errors must be distinct values")
}

package cmd

import (
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestBeadCreateRejectsDependsOnParentAncestor(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	rootOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Root", "--type", "task")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	rootID := strings.TrimSpace(rootOut)
	require.NotEmpty(t, rootID)

	parentOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Parent", "--type", "task", "--parent", rootID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	parentID := strings.TrimSpace(parentOut)
	require.NotEmpty(t, parentID)

	t.Run("direct-parent", func(t *testing.T) {
		_, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Child direct", "--type", "task", "--parent", parentID, "--depends-on", parentID)
		require.Error(t, err)
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "ancestor in the parent chain")
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), parentID)
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), parentID+" -> "+rootID)
	})

	t.Run("grandparent-ancestor", func(t *testing.T) {
		_, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Child ancestor", "--type", "task", "--parent", parentID, "--depends-on", rootID)
		require.Error(t, err)
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "ancestor in the parent chain")
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), rootID)
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), parentID+" -> "+rootID)
	})
}

data.text: There’s already a partial guard in `DepAdd`; the gap is whether `Create` builds the same parent-chain check before persisting, and whether the error text is consistent enough for both CLIs. I’m reading that path now.

package cmd

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestBeadLint_CLI_PrintsClassification(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	// Create a bead with mixed AC text: 1 test-name (verifiable) + 2 prose.
	out, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Lint test bead",
		"--acceptance", "1. TestFooBar passes\n2. improve clarity\n3. better error messages\n")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	beadID := strings.TrimSpace(out)
	require.NotEmpty(t, beadID)

	// Human-readable output must include kind labels.
	out, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "lint", beadID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, out, "test-name")
	assert.Contains(t, out, "prose")
	assert.Contains(t, out, "bead lint for "+beadID)

	// JSON output must be parseable and include per-AC items.
	out, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "lint", beadID, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Equal(t, float64(2), result["prose_count"])

	assert.Len(t, items, 3)

4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Don't mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- 'ddx bead dep add <bead> <dep>' HARD-REJECTS the edge when <dep> is <bead>'s parent OR any ancestor in the parent chain. Error message names the offending ancestor and prints the relevant parent-chain. Test: ddx_bead_dep_add_rejects_parent_ancestor.
- 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.     </description>     <acceptance> 1.
